arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سَالِمٌ، عَنْ أَبِي الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بَعْدَ الْمَكْتُوبَةِ مِثْلَهَا

english.narrator: Sālim → Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Abū Ḥamzah → Ibrāhīm → ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Sālim narrated to us, from Abū al-Aḥwaṣ, from Abū Ḥamzah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “‘Abd Allāh used to dislike praying a similar prayer after the prescribed prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَكْرَهُونَ أَنْ يُصَلُّوا بَعْدَ الْمَكْتُوبَةِ مِثْلَهَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “They used to dislike praying a similar prayer after the prescribed prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ خَرَشَةَ بْنِ الْحُرِّ، عَنْ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بَعْدَ الْمَكْتُوبَةِ مِثْلَهَا

english.narrator: Hushaym → Al-A‘mash → Sulaymān ibn Mushir → Kharashah ibn al-Ḥurr → ‘Umar
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Al-A‘mash, from Sulaymān ibn Mushir, from Kharashah ibn al-Ḥurr, from ‘Umar, that he disliked praying a similar prayer after the prescribed prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: الْأَبْعَدُ، فَالْأَبْعَدُ مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ أَعْظَمُ أَجْرًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Ibn Abi Dhi’b → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Mihran → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sa‘d → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki‘ narrated to us from Ibn Abi Dhi’b, from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Mihran, from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sa‘d, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “The further, then the further from the mosque, the greater the reward.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ حَارِثَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: مِنْ حِينِ يَخْرُجُ أَحَدُكُمْ مِنْ بَيْتِهِ إِلَى مَسْجِدِهِ، فَرِجْلٌ تَكْتُبُ حَسَنَةً، وَالْأُخْرَى تَحُطُّ سَيِّئَةً

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Ibn Abi Dhi’b → Al-Aswad ibn al-‘Ala’ ibn Harithah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Ibn Abi Dhi’b, from Al-Aswad ibn al-‘Ala’ ibn Harithah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “From the moment one of you leaves his house to go to his mosque, one foot writes a good deed, and the other erases a bad deed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ أَخِيهِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: كَانَتْ مَنَازِلُنَا قَاصِيَةً، فَأَرَدْنَا أَنْ نَتَقَرَّبَ مِنْ مَسْجِدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَذَكَرْنَا ذَلِكَ لَهُ، فَقَالَ: لَا تَفْعَلُوهَا ائْتُوهَا كَمَا كُنْتُمْ، مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ يَتَوَضَّأُ فَيُحْسِنُ الْوُضُوءَ، ثُمَّ يَخْرُجُ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ إِلَّا كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِكُلِّ خُطْوَةٍ حَسَنَةً، وَحَطَّ عَنْهُ بِهَا سَيِّئَةً

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Musa ibn ‘Ubaydah → His Brother → Jabir
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Musa ibn ‘Ubaydah, from his brother, from Jabir, who said: “Our homes were far away, so we wanted to move closer to the Mosque of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. We mentioned that to him, and he said: ‘Do not do it, stay where you are. There is no believer who performs ablution well, then goes out to the mosque, except that Allah writes for him a good deed for every step, and removes from him a bad deed by it.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أنا حُمَيْدٌ الطَّوِيلُ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، أَنَّ بَنِي سَلِمَةَ أَرَادُوا أَنْ يَتَحَوَّلُوا عَنْ مَنَازِلِهِمْ قَرِيبًا مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَكَرِهَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ تَعْرَى الْمَدِينَةُ، فَقَالَ: يَا بَنِي سَلِمَةَ، أَلَا تَحْتَسِبُونَ آثَارَكُمْ؟ قَالُوا: بَلَى فَثَبَتُوا

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Humayd al-Tawil → Anas ibn Malik
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, saying: Humayd al-Tawil informed us from Anas ibn Malik, that Banu Salamah wanted to move from their homes closer to the mosque. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ disliked that the outskirts of Madinah be left empty, so he said: “O Banu Salamah, do you not seek reward for your footsteps?” They said: “Yes.” So they stayed put.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّ بَنِي سَلِمَةَ كَانَتْ دُورُهُمْ قَاصِيَةً عَنِ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَهَمُّوا أَنْ يَتَحَوَّلُوا قَرِيبًا مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ فَيَشْهَدُوا الصَّلَاةَ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقَالَ لَهُمُ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَلَا تَحْتَسِبُونَ آثَارَكُمْ يَا بَنِي سَلِمَةَ؟ فَثَبَتُوا فِي دِيَارِهِمْ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, that the houses of Banu Salamah were far from the mosque, so they intended to move closer to the mosque to attend prayer with the Prophet ﷺ. The Prophet ﷺ said to them: “Do you not seek reward for your footsteps, O Banu Salamah?” So they stayed in their homes.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنِ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ النَّهْدِيِّ، عَنْ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَجُلٌ بِالْمَدِينَةِ، مَا أَعْلَمُ أَحَدًا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ مِمَّنْ يُصَلِّي الْقِبْلَةَ أَبْعَدَ مَنْزِلًا مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ مِنْهُ، فَكَانَ يَشْهَدُ الصَّلَاةَ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَقِيلَ لَهُ: ابْتَغَيْتَ حِمَارًا تَرْكَبُهُ فِي الرَّمْضَاءِ وَالظُّلْمَةِ؟ فَقَالَ: وَاللَّهِ مَا يَسُرُّنِي أَنَّ مَنْزِلِي يَلْزَقُ الْمَسْجِدَ، فَذَكَرَ ذَلِكَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، كَيْمَا يُكْتَبُ خُطَايَ، وَإِقْبَالِي وَإِدْبَارِي وَرُجُوعِي إِلَى أَهْلِي، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَبْطَأَكَ اللَّهُ ذَلِكَ، وَأَعْطَاكَ مَا احْتَسَبْتَ أَجْمَعَ أَوْ كَمَا قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Al-Taymi → Abu ‘Uthman al-Nahdi → Ubayy ibn Ka‘b
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us from Al-Taymi, from Abu ‘Uthman al-Nahdi, from Ubayy ibn Ka‘b, who said: There was a man in Madinah—I do not know anyone from the people of Madinah who prays facing the Qiblah whose house was further from the mosque than his. Yet he used to attend prayer with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. It was said to him: “Why don’t you get a donkey to ride in the intense heat and darkness?” He said: “By Allah, it would not please me if my house were right next to the mosque.” That was mentioned to the Prophet ﷺ, and he said: “O Messenger of Allah, [I do this] so that my steps, my coming, my going, and my returning to my family may be recorded.” The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Allah has granted you that delay and given you all that you sought reward for,” or as the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ ابْنَ أَبِي لَيْلَى، فَقُلْتُ: بَنُو سَلِمَةَ أَرَادُوا أَنْ يَتَحَوَّلُوا قَرِيبًا مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَذُكِرَ عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: فَإِنَّ بِكُلِّ خُطْوَةٍ حَسَنَةً

english.narrator: ‘Ali ibn Hashim → Ibn Abi Layla → Abu al-Zubayr → Jabir
english.text: ‘Ali ibn Hashim narrated to us, saying: I asked Ibn Abi Layla, saying: “Banu Salamah wanted to move closer to the mosque.” It was mentioned from Abu al-Zubayr, from Jabir, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “For every step there is a good deed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الْحَجَّاجِ بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: أَيَعْجَزُ أَحَدُكُمْ إِذَا صَلَّى أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمَ أَوْ يَتَأَخَّرَ، أَوْ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ أَوْ عَنْ شِمَالِهِ يَعْنِي السُّبْحَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Isma‘il ibn Ibrahim → Layth → Al-Hajjaj ibn ‘Ubayd → Ibrahim ibn Isma‘il → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Isma‘il ibn Ibrahim narrated to us from Layth, from Al-Hajjaj ibn ‘Ubayd, from Ibrahim ibn Isma‘il, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: “Is one of you incapable, when he prays, of moving forward or backward, or to his right or to his left?” Meaning for the voluntary prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، وَابْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، وَأَبَا سَعِيدٍ، وَابْنَ عُمَرَ، كَانُوا يَقُولُونَ: لَا يَتَطَوَّعُ حَتَّى يَتَحَوَّلَ مِنْ مَكَانِهِ الَّذِي صَلَّى فِيهِ الْفَرِيضَةَ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyub → ‘Ata’ → Ibn ‘Abbas/Ibn al-Zubayr/Abu Sa‘id/Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ayyub, from ‘Ata’, that Ibn ‘Abbas, Ibn al-Zubayr, Abu Sa‘id, and Ibn ‘Umar used to say: “He should not pray voluntary prayers until he moves from the place where he prayed the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: لَا يَتَطَوَّعُ حَتَّى يَنْهَضَ خُطْوَةً، أَوْ خُطْوَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Ibn Idris → Husayn → ‘Amir
english.text: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Husayn, from ‘Amir, who said: “He should not pray voluntary prayers until he moves a step or two.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَبِي إِذَا صَلَّى الْمَكْتُوبَةَ مَالَ عَنْ مَكَانِهِ، فَسَبَّحَ

english.narrator: Hatim ibn Isma‘il → Hisham → His Father
english.text: Hatim ibn Isma‘il narrated to us from Hisham, who said: “When my father prayed the obligatory prayer, he would shift from his place and perform Tasbih (supererogatory prayer).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي بَحْرٍ، عَنْ شَيْخٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودَ عَنْ الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي فِي مَكَانِهِ الَّذِي صَلَّى فِيهِ الْفَرِيضَةَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Abu Bahr → Shaykh → Ibn Mas‘ud
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Abu Bahr, from a shaykh, who said: Ibn Mas‘ud was asked about a man praying in the same place where he prayed the obligatory prayer. He said: “There is no harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُصَلِّي سُبْحَتَهُ مَكَانَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyub → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ayyub, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to pray his supererogatory prayer in his place.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْقَاسِمَ، وَسَالِمًا يُصَلِّيَانِ الْفَرِيضَةَ، ثُمَّ يَتَطَوَّعَانِ فِي مَكَانِهِمَا، قَالَ: وَأَنْبَأَنِي نَافِعٌ، أَنَّ ابْنَ عُمَرَ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir → ‘Ubayd Allah ibn ‘Umar → Al-Qasim/Salim
english.text: Mu‘tamir narrated to us from ‘Ubayd Allah ibn ‘Umar, who said: “I saw Al-Qasim and Salim praying the obligatory prayer, then praying voluntary prayers in their places.” He said: And Nafi‘ informed me that Ibn ‘Umar saw no harm in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً، عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَتَطَوَّعُ فِي مَكَانِهِ، فَقَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Mis‘ar → ‘Ata’
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Mis‘ar, who said: I asked ‘Ata’ about a man praying voluntary prayers in his place. He said: “There is no harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَمُحَمَّدٍ أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا يُصَلِّيَانِ التَّطَوُّعَ فِي مَكَانِهِمَا الَّذِي يُصَلِّيَانِ فِيهِ الْفَرِيضَةَ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Hisham → Al-Hasan/Muhammad
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan and Muhammad, that they used to pray voluntary prayers in the same place where they prayed the obligatory prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، قَالَ: غَيْرُ الْإِمَامِ إِنْ شَاءَ لَمْ يَتَحَوَّلْ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hisham → Qatadah → Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Hisham, from Qatadah, from Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib, who said: “Other than the Imam, if he wishes, he does not move.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مَيْسَرَةَ بْنِ الْمِنْهَالِ، عَنْ عَمَّارِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: إِذَا سَلَّمَ الْإِمَامُ، لَمْ يَتَطَوَّعْ حَتَّى يَتَحَوَّلَ مِنْ مَكَانِهِ، أَوْ يَفْصِلَ بَيْنَهُمَا بِكَلَامٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sharik → Maysarah ibn al-Minhal → ‘Ammar ibn ‘Abd Allah → ‘Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us from Maysarah ibn al-Minhal, from ‘Ammar ibn ‘Abd Allah, from ‘Ali, who said: “When the Imam says the Taslim, he should not pray voluntary prayers until he moves from his place or separates between them with speech.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرِ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ إِذَا صَلَّى الْإِمَامُ أَنْ يَتَطَوَّعَ فِي مَكَانِهِ، وَلَمْ يَرَ بِهِ لِغَيْرِ الْإِمَامِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar → Hajjaj → Abu Ishaq → Al-Sha‘bi → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Khalid al-Ahmar narrated to us from Hajjaj, from Abu Ishaq, from Al-Sha‘bi, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he disliked for the Imam to pray voluntary prayers in his place if he had prayed [the obligatory prayer], but he saw no harm in it for someone other than the Imam.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مُهَاجِرٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ لِلْإِمَامِ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ فِي مَكَانِهِ الَّذِي صَلَّى فِيهِ الْفَرِيضَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → Hajjaj → Ibrahim ibn Muhajir → Mujahid → ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Amr
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Hajjaj, from Ibrahim ibn Muhajir, from Mujahid, from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Amr, that he disliked for the Imam to pray in the place where he prayed the obligatory prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَسْتَحِبُّ لِلْإِمَامِ إِذَا صَلَّى أَنْ لَا يَتَطَوَّعَ فِي مَكَانِهِ الَّذِي صَلَّى فِيهِ أَوْ قَالَ: كَانَ يَكْرَهُهُ

english.narrator: Hafs → Hajjaj → Al-Hakam → Ibn Abi Layla
english.text: Hafs narrated to us from Hajjaj, from Al-Hakam, from Ibn Abi Layla, that he used to prefer for the Imam, when he prayed, not to pray voluntary prayers in the place where he prayed. Or he said: He disliked it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وَالْحَسَنِ أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا يُعْجِبُهُمَا إِذَا سَلَّمَ الْإِمَامُ أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمَ

english.narrator: ‘Ali ibn Mushir → Ibn Abi ‘Arubah → Qatadah → Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib/Al-Hasan
english.text: ‘Ali ibn Mushir narrated to us from Ibn Abi ‘Arubah, from Qatadah, from Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib and Al-Hasan, that they used to like for the Imam to move forward when he said the Taslim.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ أَنَّهُ يُكْرَهُ لِلْإِمَامِ أَنْ يَتَطَوَّعَ فِي مَكَانِهِ الَّذِي صَلَّى فِيهِ الْفَرِيضَةَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, that it is disliked for the Imam to pray voluntary prayers in the place where he prayed the obligatory prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنِ الْمِنْهَالِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ عَبَّادِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: لَا يَتَطَوَّعُ الْإِمَامُ فِي الْمَكَانِ الَّذِي أَمَّ فِيهِ الْقَوْمَ، حَتَّى يَتَحَوَّلَ أَوْ يَفْصِلَ بِكَلَامٍ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Maysarah → Al-Minhal ibn ‘Amr → ‘Abbad ibn ‘Abd Allah → ‘Ali
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Maysarah, from Al-Minhal ibn ‘Amr, from ‘Abbad ibn ‘Abd Allah, from ‘Ali, who said: “The Imam should not pray voluntary prayers in the place where he led the people until he moves or separates with speech.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ الدَّسْتُوَائِيِّ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، قَالَ: الْإِمَامُ يَتَحَوَّلُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hisham al-Dastuwa’i → Qatadah → Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Hisham al-Dastuwa’i, from Qatadah, from Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib, who said: “The Imam should move.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا صَلَّى الْإِمَامُ الْمَكْتُوبَةَ، ثُمَّ أَرَادَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ التَّطَوُّعَ، تَنَحَّى مِنْ مَكَانِهِ الَّذِي صَلَّى فِيهِ الْفَرِيضَةَ

english.narrator: Jarir → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Jarir narrated to us from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: “If the Imam prays the obligatory prayer, then wishes to pray the voluntary prayer, he should move aside from his place where he prayed the obligatory prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَسْتَحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمُوا فِي الصَّلَاةِ وَلَا يَتَأَخَّرُوا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abd al-A‘la → Khalid → Abu Qilabah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Khalid, from Abu Qilabah, who said: “They used to prefer moving forward in prayer and not backward.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِمُحَمَّدٍ: الرَّجُلُ يَتَقَدَّمُ إِلَى الصَّفِّ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، قَالَ: لَا أَعْلَمُ بَأْسًا أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمَ خُطْوَةً، أَوْ خُطْوَتَيْنِ وَقَالَ فِي الَّذِي يُصَلِّي الصَّفَّ مُعْتَرِضًا: لَا أَدْرِي مَا هُوَ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Abi ‘Adi → Ibn ‘Awn → Muhammad
english.text: Muhammad ibn Abi ‘Adi narrated to us from Ibn ‘Awn, who said: I said to Muhammad [Ibn Sirin]: “A man moves forward to the row during prayer.” He said: “I see no harm in moving forward a step or two.” And he said regarding the one who prays while the row is perpendicular: “I do not know what it is.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ مَعَهُ الشَّيْءُ فَيَضَعُهُ، فَيُصَلِّيَ، ثُمَّ يَبْدُو لَهُ أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَأْخُذَهُ ثُمَّ يَتَقَدَّمَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → ‘Abd al-Malik → ‘Ata’
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from ‘Abd al-Malik, from ‘Ata’, regarding a man who has something with him, places it down, prays, then decides to move forward. He said: “There is no harm in him taking it and then moving forward.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، وَسُفْيَانَ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، قَالَ: "كَانَ يُقَالُ: تَقَدَّمُوا تَقَدَّمُوا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Mis‘ar/Sufyan → Sa‘d ibn Ibrahim → ‘Urwah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Mis‘ar and Sufyan, from Sa‘d ibn Ibrahim, from ‘Urwah, who said: “It used to be said: ‘Move forward, move forward.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الشَّعْبِيَّ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ كَانَ يُصَلِّي وَبَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ قَوْمٌ يُصَلُّونَ، فَانْصَرَفُوا، قَالَ: يَتَقَدَّمُ إِلَى الْحَائِطِ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: أَفَيَقْرَأُ وَهُوَ يَمْشِي؟ قَالَ: لَا حَتَّى يَنْتَهِيَ إِلَى الْمَكَانِ الَّذِي يَقُومُ فِيهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Isma‘il ibn Salim → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Isma‘il ibn Salim, who said: I asked Al-Sha‘bi about a man who was praying and there were people praying in front of him, then they left. He said: “He should move forward to the wall in front of him.” I said: “Does he recite while walking?” He said: “No, until he reaches the place where he will stand.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ الْبُنَانِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ إِلَى جَنْبِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي بِاللَّيْلِ تَطَوُّعًا، فَمَرَّ بِآيَةٍ، فَقَالَ: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ النَّارِ، وَوَيْلٌ لِأَهْلِ النَّارِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Ali ibn Hashim → Ibn Abi Layla → Thabit al-Bunani → Ibn Abi Layla → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Ali ibn Hashim narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from Thabit al-Bunani, from Ibn Abi Layla, from his father, who said: I prayed next to the Prophet ﷺ while he was offering voluntary prayers at night. He passed by a verse and said: “I seek refuge in Allah from the Fire, and woe to the people of the Fire.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّهَا مَرَّتْ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: {فَمَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا وَوَقَانَا عَذَابَ السَّمُومِ} [الطور: 27] فَقَالَتْ: اللَّهُمَّ مُنَّ عَلَيْنَا، وَقِنَا عَذَابَ السَّمُومِ، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْبَرُّ الرَّحِيمُ فَقِيلَ لِلْأَعْمَشِ: فِي الصَّلَاةِ؟ فَقَالَ: فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Al-A‘mash → Abu al-Duha → Masruq → ‘Aishah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-A‘mash narrated to us from Abu al-Duha, from Masruq, from ‘Aishah, that she passed by this verse: {So Allah conferred favor upon us and protected us from the punishment of the Scorching Fire} [At-Tur: 27]. She said: “O Allah, confer favor upon us and protect us from the punishment of the Scorching Fire; indeed, You are the Beneficent, the Merciful.” It was said to Al-A‘mash: “In prayer?” He said: “In prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ عَبَّادِ بْنِ حَمْزَةَ، قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَسْمَاءَ وَهِيَ تَقْرَأُ: {فَمَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا وَوَقَانَا عَذَابَ السَّمُومِ} [الطور: 27] قَالَ: فَوَقَفَتْ عَلَيْهَا، فَجَعَلَتْ تَسْتَعِيذُ وَتَدْعُو قَالَ عَبَّادٌ: فَذَهَبْتُ إِلَى السُّوقِ، فَقَضَيْتُ حَاجَتِي، ثُمَّ رَجَعْتُ وَهِيَ فِيهَا بَعْدُ تَسْتَعِيذُ وَتَدْعُو

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → Hisham ibn ‘Urwah → ‘Abd al-Wahhab → ‘Abbad ibn Hamzah
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us from Hisham ibn ‘Urwah, from ‘Abd al-Wahhab, from his grandfather ‘Abbad ibn Hamzah, who said: I entered upon Asma’ while she was reciting: {So Allah conferred favor upon us and protected us from the punishment of the Scorching Fire} [At-Tur: 27]. He said: “She stopped at it and began seeking refuge and supplicating.” ‘Abbad said: I went to the market, fulfilled my need, then returned, and she was still at it, seeking refuge and supplicating.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عِيسَى، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: إِذَا مَرَّ أَحَدُكُمْ فِي الصَّلَاةِ بِذِكْرِ النَّارِ، فَلْيَسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ النَّارِ، وَإِذَا مَرَّ بِذِكْرِ الْجَنَّةِ، فَلْيَسْأَلِ اللَّهَ الْجَنَّةَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → ‘Isa → Al-Sha‘bi → ‘Abd Allah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from ‘Isa, from Al-Sha‘bi, who said: ‘Abd Allah said: “If one of you passes by a mention of the Fire in prayer, let him seek refuge in Allah from the Fire. And if he passes by a mention of Paradise, let him ask Allah for Paradise.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا إِذَا مَرَّ بِآيَةٍ أَنْ يَسْأَلَ وَأَنَّ ابْنَ سِيرِينَ، كَرِهَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, that he saw no harm in asking if one passed by a verse. And that Ibn Sirin disliked it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، وَابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنِ الْمُسْتَوْرِدِ بْنِ الْأَحْنَفِ، عَنْ صِلَةَ، عَنْ حُذَيْفَةَ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَكَانَ إِذَا مَرَّ بِآيَةٍ فِيهَا تَسْبِيحٌ سَبَّحَ، وَإِذَا مَرَّ بِسُؤَالٍ سَأَلَ، وَإِذَا مَرَّ بِتَعَوُّذٍ تَعَوَّذَ

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah/Ibn Numayr → Al-A‘mash → Sa‘id ibn ‘Ubaydah → Al-Mustawrid ibn al-Ahnaf → Silah → Hudhayfah
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah and Ibn Numayr narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, from Sa‘id ibn ‘Ubaydah, from Al-Mustawrid ibn al-Ahnaf, from Silah, from Hudhayfah, who said: “I prayed with the Prophet ﷺ. Whenever he passed by a verse containing glorification, he glorified; whenever he passed by a request, he asked; and whenever he passed by a seeking of refuge, he sought refuge.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَسْمَعُ الرَّجُلَ وَأَنَا أُصَلِّي يَقُولُ: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ} [الأحزاب: 56] أَأُصَلِّي عَلَيْهِ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ إِنْ شِئْتَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us from Mughirah, who said: I said to Ibrahim: “I hear a man while I am praying saying: {Indeed, Allah and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet} [Al-Ahzab: 56]. Should I send blessings upon him?” He said: “Yes, if you wish.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: "إِذَا قَالَ الرَّجُلُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ} [الأحزاب: 56] فَلْيُصَلِّ عَلَيْهِ" قَالَ: وَقَالَ ابْنُ سِيرِينَ: كَانُوا إِذَا قَرَءُوا الْقُرْآنَ لَمْ يَخْلِطُوا بِهِ مَا لَيْسَ مِنْهُ، وَيَمْضُونَ كَمَا هُمْ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, who said: “If a man says in prayer: {Indeed, Allah and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet. O you who have believed, ask [Allah to confer] blessing upon him} [Al-Ahzab: 56], let him send blessings upon him.” He said: And Ibn Sirin said: “When they recited the Qur’an, they did not mix it with what is not from it, and they would continue as they were.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: الرَّجُلُ يَمُرُّ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ: {إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَمَلَائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ} [الأحزاب: 56] أَيُصَلِّي عَلَيْهِ؟ قَالَ: يَمُرُّ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Jabir → ‘Amir
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Jabir, from ‘Amir, who said: I said to him: “A man passes by this verse in prayer: {Indeed, Allah and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet} [Al-Ahzab: 56]. Should he send blessings upon him?” He said: “He passes on.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا خَالِدُ بْنُ الْحَارِثِ، وَعَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَطَنٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، فِي الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ لَا يَمْنَعُهَا ذَلِكَ مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Khalid ibn al-Harith/‘Abdah ibn Sulayman → Sa‘id → Qatan → ‘Ata’ → ‘Aishah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Khalid ibn al-Harith and ‘Abdah ibn Sulayman narrated to us from Sa‘id, from Qatan, from ‘Ata’, from ‘Aishah, regarding the pregnant woman who sees blood: “That does not prevent her from prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَامِعِ بْنِ أَبِي رَاشِدٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ قَالَ: تَتَوَضَّأُ وَتُصَلِّي

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Jami‘ ibn Abi Rashid → ‘Ata’
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Jami‘ ibn Abi Rashid, from ‘Ata’, regarding the pregnant woman who sees blood. He said: “She performs ablution and prays.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، قَالَ: كَتَبْتُ إِلَى نَافِعٍ، أَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ، فَكَتَبَ إِلَيَّ سَأَلْتُ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنَ يَسَارٍ، عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ تَرَى الدَّمَ فِي غَيْرِ حَيْضٍ وَلَا نِفَاسٍ، قَالَ: تَغْتَسِلُ، وَتَسْتَثْفِرُ بِثَوْبٍ، وَتُصَلِّي

english.narrator: Isra’il → Ayyub → Nafi‘/Sulayman ibn Yasar
english.text: Isra’il narrated to us from Ayyub, who said: I wrote to Nafi‘ asking him about the pregnant woman who sees blood. He wrote back to me saying: I asked Sulayman ibn Yasar about a woman who sees blood outside of menstruation and post-partum bleeding. He said: “She bathes, binds herself with a cloth, and prays.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، وَعَطَاءٍ، فِي الْحُبْلَى تَرَى الدَّمَ عَبِيطًا تَغْتَسِلُ وَتُصَلِّي

english.narrator: Hafs ibn Ghiyath → Layth → Al-Sha‘bi/‘Ata’
english.text: Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated to us from Layth, from Al-Sha‘bi and ‘Ata’, regarding the pregnant woman who sees fresh blood: “She bathes and prays.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ قَالَ: تَصْنَعُ كَمَا تَصْنَعُ الْمُسْتَحَاضَةُ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, regarding the pregnant woman who sees blood. He said: “She does as the woman with Istihadah does.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ قَالَ: إِنْ كَانَتْ تَرَاهُ كَمَا كَانَتْ تَرَاهُ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ فِي أَقْرَائِهَا تَرَكَتِ الصَّلَاةَ، وَإِنْ كَانَ إِنَّمَا هُوَ فِي الْيَوْمِ وَالْيَوْمَيْنِ، لَمْ تَدَعِ الصَّلَاةَ

english.narrator: ‘Abbad ibn al-‘Awwam → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: ‘Abbad ibn al-‘Awwam narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, regarding the pregnant woman who sees blood. He said: “If she sees it as she used to see it before that during her periods, she leaves prayer. But if it is only for a day or two, she does not leave prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا رَأَتْهُ وَهِيَ حُبْلَى فَلْتَتَوَضَّأْ وَلْتُصَلِّ، فَإِنَّهُ لَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Al-Hasan ibn al-Hakam → Al-Hakam → Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Al-Hasan ibn al-Hakam, from Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim, who said: “If she sees it while pregnant, let her perform ablution and pray, for it is nothing.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، فِي الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ قَالَ: لَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ وَقَالَ حَمَّادٌ: هِيَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الْمُسْتَحَاضَةِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd Allah ibn Numayr → Shu‘bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: ‘Abd Allah ibn Numayr narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from Al-Hakam, regarding the pregnant woman who sees blood. He said: “It is nothing.” And Hammad said: “She is in the status of the woman with Istihadah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ هَرِمٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ جَابِرُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ أَيَمْنَعُهَا ذَلِكَ مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ؟ فَقَالَ: إِنَّمَا يَمْنَعُ مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ وَالصَّوْمِ الْحَيْضُ، وَهَذَا الْفَيْضُ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Habib → ‘Amr ibn Harim → Jabir ibn Zayd
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Habib, from ‘Amr ibn Harim, who said: Jabir ibn Zayd was asked about the pregnant woman who sees blood, does that prevent her from prayer? He said: “Only menstruation prevents prayer and fasting, and this is overflow.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الزُّهْرِيَّ عَنِ الْحَامِلِ تَرَى الدَّمَ، قَالَ: ذَا الدَّمِ يَكُفُّ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn Hubab → Malik ibn Anas → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Zayd ibn Hubab narrated to us from Malik ibn Anas, who said: I asked Al-Zuhri about the pregnant woman who sees blood. He said: “That blood stops one from prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ مِنْدَلٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، وَالْحَكَمِ، وَحَمَّادٍ، قَالُوا: لَا يَجْتَمِعُ حَبَلٌ وَحَيْضٌ، فَإِذَا رَأَتِ الْحَامِلُ الدَّمَ، فَلْتُصَلِّ

english.narrator: Humayd ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman → Mindal → Ibn Abi Ishaq → ‘Ikrimah/Al-Hakam/Hammad
english.text: Humayd ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman narrated to us from Mindal, from Ibn Abi Ishaq, from ‘Ikrimah, Al-Hakam, and Hammad, who said: “Pregnancy and menstruation do not combine. So if a pregnant woman sees blood, let her pray.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: إِذَا رَأَتِ الدَّمَ عَلَى الْوَلَدِ، أَمْسَكَتْ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, that he used to say: “If she sees blood on the child, she refrains from prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الْمَرْأَةِ تَرَى الدَّمَ وَهِيَ تُطْلَقُ قَالَ: تَصْنَعُ مَا تَصْنَعُ الْمُسْتَحَاضَةُ

english.narrator: Ibn Mubarak → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Ibn Mubarak narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, regarding a woman seeing blood while in labor. He said: “She does what the woman with Istihadah does.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الْمَرْأَةِ تَرَى الدَّمَ وَهِيَ تَمْخُضُ قَالَ: هِيَ حَيْضٌ لَا تُصَلِّي

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Al-Hasan ibn al-Hakam → Al-Hakam → Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Al-Hasan ibn al-Hakam, from Al-Hakam, from Ibrahim, regarding a woman seeing blood while in labor. He said: “It is menstruation; she does not pray.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ هَمَّامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا رَأَتِ الدَّمَ عَلَى رَأْسِ الْوَلَدِ، أَمْسَكَتْ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: ‘Abbad ibn al-‘Awwam → Hammam → Al-Hasan
english.text: ‘Abbad ibn al-‘Awwam narrated to us from Hammam, from Al-Hasan, who said: “If she sees blood on the head of the child, she refrains from prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا مُجَالِدٌ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: خَرَجَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِلَى بَدْرٍ، فَاسْتَخْلَفَ ابْنَ أُمِّ مَكْتُومٍ عَلَى الْمَدِينَةِ، فَكَانَ يَؤُمُّهُمْ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Usamah → Mujalid → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us, saying: Mujalid informed us from Al-Sha‘bi, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out to Badr and appointed Ibn Umm Maktum as successor over Madinah. He used to lead them in prayer while he was blind.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ اسْتَخْلَفَ ابْنَ أُمِّ مَكْتُومٍ، فَكَانَ يَؤُمُّ النَّاسَ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Yunus → Abu Ishaq → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Yunus, from Abu Ishaq, from Al-Sha‘bi, that the Prophet ﷺ appointed Ibn Umm Maktum as successor, and he used to lead the people in prayer while he was blind.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّ أُنَاسًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ كَانُوا يَؤُمُّونَ وَهُمْ عُمْيَانٌ، مِنْهُمْ عِتْبَانُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ، وَمُعَاذُ ابْنُ عَفْرَاءَ، وَابْنُ أُمِّ مَكْتُومٍ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Ma‘mar → Al-Zuhri
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhri, that some people among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ used to lead prayers while they were blind, among them ‘Itban ibn Malik, Mu‘adh ibn ‘Afra’, and Ibn Umm Maktum.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: كَانَ نَاسٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَدْرٍ يَؤُمُّونَ فِي مَسَاجِدِهِمْ، بَعْدَمَا ذَهَبَتْ أَبْصَارُهُمْ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Ibn Abi Dhi’b → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Ibn Abi Dhi’b, from Al-Zuhri, who said: “People from the participants of Badr used to lead prayers in their mosques after they lost their sight.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: دَخَلْنَا عَلَى جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى، فَجَاءَ وَقْتُ الصَّلَاةِ، فَقَامَ فِي نِسَاجَةٍ مُلْتَحِفًا كُلَّمَا وَضَعَهَا عَلَى مَنْكِبَيْهِ رَجَعَ طَرَفَاهَا إِلَيْهِ مِنْ صِغَرِهَا، وَرِدَاؤُهُ إِلَى جَنْبِهِ عَلَى الْمِشْجَبِ، فَصَلَّى بِنَا

english.narrator: Hatim ibn Isma‘il → Ja‘far → His Father → Jabir ibn ‘Abd Allah
english.text: Hatim ibn Isma‘il narrated to us from Ja‘far, from his father, who said: “We entered upon Jabir ibn ‘Abd Allah while he was blind. The time for prayer came, so he stood up wrapped in a woven garment. Whenever he placed it on his shoulders, its ends would slip back to him due to its small size, while his cloak was beside him on the hanger. He led us in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عَامِرٍ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَأَلَ الْحَسَنَ، أَأُمُّ قَوْمِي وَأَنَا أَعْمَى؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Sa‘id → Abu ‘Amir → A man → Al-Hasan
english.text: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us from Abu ‘Amir, that a man asked Al-Hasan: “Should I lead my people in prayer while I am blind?” He said: “Yes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْحُ بْنُ عُبَادَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، سُئِلَ عَنِ الْأَعْمَى يَؤُمُّ؟ قَالَ: فَقَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ أَفْقَهَهُمْ

english.narrator: Rawh ibn ‘Ubadah → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Rawh ibn ‘Ubadah narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, who was asked about the blind person leading prayer. He said: “If he is the most knowledgeable of them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ الْأَعْمَى

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Hammad → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: “There is no harm in a blind person leading prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: نا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: أَمَّنَا ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan → Sharik → Abu Ishaq → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us from Abu Ishaq, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, who said: “Ibn ‘Abbas led us in prayer while he was blind.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنِ ابْنٍ لَعُمَرَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَعْمَى كَانَ يَؤُمُّ بَنِي خَطْمَةَ فِي زَمَنِ عُمَرَ

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → Hisham ibn ‘Urwah → Son of ‘Umar → His Father
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us from Hisham ibn ‘Urwah, from a son of ‘Umar, from his father, that a blind man used to lead Banu Khatmah in prayer during the time of ‘Umar.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مُهَاجِرٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ الْبَرَاءُ يُصَلِّي بِنَا وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan → Sharik → Muhajir → Al-Bara’
english.text: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan narrated to us, saying: Sharik narrated to us from Muhajir, who said: “Al-Bara’ used to lead us in prayer while he was blind.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ مَحْمُودِ بْنِ الرَّبِيعِ، عَنْ عِتْبَانَ بْنِ مَالِكٍ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَؤُمُّ قَوْمَهُ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Ma‘mar → Al-Zuhri → Mahmud ibn al-Rabi‘ → ‘Itban ibn Malik
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhri, from Mahmud ibn al-Rabi‘, from ‘Itban ibn Malik, that he used to lead his people in prayer while he was blind.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا خَالِدُ بْنُ مَخْلَدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ مَحْمُودِ بْنِ الرَّبِيعِ، عَنْ عِتْبَانَ بْنِ مَالِكٍ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَؤُمُّ قَوْمَهُ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Khalid ibn Makhlad → Malik ibn Anas → Ibn Shihab → Mahmud ibn al-Rabi‘ → ‘Itban ibn Malik
english.text: Khalid ibn Makhlad narrated to us, saying: Malik ibn Anas narrated to us from Ibn Shihab, from Mahmud ibn al-Rabi‘, from ‘Itban ibn Malik, that he used to lead his people in prayer while he was blind.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: كَانَ إِمَامُ بَنِي خَطْمَةَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Hisham → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Hisham narrated to us from his father, who said: “The Imam of Banu Khatmah was blind.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: أَمَّنَا جَابِرٌ بَعْدَمَا ذَهَبَ بَصَرُهُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hammad ibn Zayd → ‘Amr → Abu Ja‘far → Jabir
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Hammad ibn Zayd narrated to us from ‘Amr, from Abu Ja‘far, who said: “Jabir led us in prayer after he lost his sight.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلَ الْحَكَمُ بْنُ عُتَيْبَةَ الْقَاسِمَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَى يَؤُمُّ وَتَجُوزُ شَهَادَتُهُ؟ فَقَالَ: مَا يَمْنَعُهُ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ وَتَجُوزُ شَهَادَتُهُ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Fudayl → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → Al-Hakam ibn ‘Utaybah → Al-Qasim
english.text: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Yahya ibn Sa‘id, who said: Al-Hakam ibn ‘Utaybah asked Al-Qasim about a blind person leading prayer and whether his testimony is accepted. He said: “What prevents him from leading prayer and his testimony being accepted?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِي، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَطِيَّةَ، قَالَ: أَمَّنَا الْمُسَيِّبُ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → ‘Amr ibn ‘Atiyyah → Al-Musayyib
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from ‘Amr ibn ‘Atiyyah, who said: “Al-Musayyib led us in prayer while he was blind.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ شَيْخٍ يُكَنَّى أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، أَنَّ ابْنَ أَبِي أَوْفَى، أَمَّهُمْ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى

english.narrator: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Hasan ibn Salih → A Shaykh nicknamed Abu ‘Abd Allah → Ibn Abi Awfa
english.text: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us from Hasan ibn Salih, from a Shaykh nicknamed Abu ‘Abd Allah, that Ibn Abi Awfa led them in prayer while he was blind.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: كَيْفَ أَؤُمُّهُمْ وَهُمْ يَعْدِلُونِي إِلَى الْقِبْلَةِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → ‘Abd al-A‘la → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from ‘Abd al-A‘la, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who said: “How can I lead them while they adjust me towards the Qiblah?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ أَبِي الْحَسْنَاءِ، عَنْ زِيَادِ النُّمَيْرِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَنَسًا عَنِ الْأَعْمَى يَؤُمُّ؟ فَقَالَ: مَا أَفْقَرَكُمْ إِلَى ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Hasan ibn Abi al-Hasna’ → Ziyad al-Numayri → Anas
english.text: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us from Hasan ibn Abi al-Hasna’, from Ziyad al-Numayri, who said: I asked Anas about the blind person leading prayer. He said: “How needy you are of that!”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ وَاصِلٍ الْأَحْدَبِ، عَنْ قَبِيصَةَ بْنِ بُرْمَةَ الْأَسَدِيِّ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: "مَا أُحِبُّ أَنْ يَكُونَ مُؤَذِّنُوكُمْ عُمْيَانَكُمْ، قَالَ: أَحْسِبُهُ قَالَ: وَلَا قُرَّاؤُكُمْ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Wasil al-Ahdab → Qabisah ibn Burmah al-Asadi → ‘Abd Allah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Wasil al-Ahdab, from Qabisah ibn Burmah al-Asadi, who said: ‘Abd Allah said: “I do not like your callers to prayer (Mu’adhdhin) to be your blind people.” He said: I think he also said: “Nor your reciters (Imams).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ مَرْزُوقٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْأَعْمَى لَا يَؤُمُّ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn al-Hubab → Isra’il → Marzuq → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Zayd ibn al-Hubab narrated to us from Isra’il, from Marzuq, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, that he said: “The blind person should not lead prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، مِنْ طَيٍّ، أَنَّ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ حَجَّ، فَصَلَّى خَلْفَ أَعْرَابِيٍّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi → Shu‘bah → Abu Ishaq → Man of Tayy → Ibn Mas‘ud
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from Abu Ishaq, from a man of Tayy, that Ibn Mas‘ud performed Hajj and prayed behind a Bedouin.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ كَهَمْسٍ، عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ الْجُرَيْرِيِّ، أَنَّ أَبَا مِجْلَزٍ كَرِهَ إِمَامَةَ الْأَعْرَابِيِّ وَأَنَّ الْحَسَنَ لَمْ يَرَ بِذَلِكَ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir → Kahmas → Al-‘Abbas al-Jurayri → Abu Mijlaz
english.text: Mu‘tamir narrated to us from Kahmas, from Al-‘Abbas al-Jurayri, that Abu Mijlaz disliked the Imamate of a Bedouin, while Al-Hasan saw no harm in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْوَارِثِ، عَنْ دَارِمٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سَالِمًا، أَيَؤُمُّ الْأَعْرَابِيُّ الْمُهَاجِرَ؟ قَالَ: وَمَا عَلَيْكُ إِذَا كَانَ رَجُلًا صَالِحًا

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Samad ibn ‘Abd al-Warith → Darim → Salim
english.text: ‘Abd al-Samad ibn ‘Abd al-Warith narrated to us from Darim, who said: I asked Salim: “Can a Bedouin lead a Muhajir in prayer?” He said: “What harm is there if he is a righteous man?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أنا مُغِيرَةُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ إِمَامَةِ الْعَبْدِ، وَالْأَعْرَابِيِّ، فَقَالَ: الْعَبْدُ، إِذَا فَقُهَ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Mughirah informed us from Ibrahim, that he was asked about the Imamate of a slave and a Bedouin. He said: “The slave, if he is knowledgeable, is more beloved to me.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ الْأَعْرَابِيُّ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Hammad → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: “There is no harm in a Bedouin leading prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، أَنَّ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ صَلَّى خَلْفَ أَعْرَابِيٍّ

english.narrator: Sufyan ibn ‘Uyaynah → Ibn Abi Najih → Mujahid → Ibn Mas‘ud
english.text: Sufyan ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, that Ibn Mas‘ud prayed behind a Bedouin.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ بُرْدٍ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَئِمَّةٌ مِنْ ذَلِكَ الْعَمَلِ يَعْنِي مِنْ أَوْلَادِ الزِّنَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Burd Abu al-‘Ala’ → Al-Zuhri
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Burd Abu al-‘Ala’, from Al-Zuhri, who said: “There were Imams from that action”—meaning illegitimate children.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ وَلَدُ الزِّنَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Hammad → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: “There is no harm in an illegitimate child leading prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ زُهَيْرِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ الْعَبْسِيِّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ الشَّعْبِيَّ، يَقُولُ: تَجُوزُ شَهَادَتُهُ وَيَؤُمُّ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Zuhayr ibn Abi Thabit al-‘Absi → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Zuhayr ibn Abi Thabit al-‘Absi, who said: I heard Al-Sha‘bi say: “His testimony is accepted and he can lead prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ إِمَامَةِ وَلَدِ الزِّنَا، فَقَالَ: إِنَّ لَنَا إِمَامًا مَا نَعْرِفُ لَهُ أَبًا

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mutarrif → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Mutarrif, from Al-Sha‘bi, that he was asked about the Imamate of an illegitimate child. He said: “We have an Imam whose father we do not know.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ وَلَدُ الزِّنَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah → Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyash → Al-A‘mash → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah narrated to us, saying: Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyash narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrahim, who said: “There is no harm in an illegitimate child leading prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً، عَنْ وَلَدِ الزِّنَا يَؤُمُّ الْقَوْمَ، فَقَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ، أَلَيْسَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ هُوَ أَكْثَرُ صَوْمًا وَصَلَاةً مِنَّا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Abu Hanifah → ‘Ata’
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Abu Hanifah narrated to us, saying: I asked ‘Ata’ about an illegitimate child leading people in prayer. He said: “There is no harm in it. Are there not among them those who fast and pray more than us?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ وَلَدُ الزِّنَا

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Mutarrif → Hammad → Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Mutarrif, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: “There is no harm in an illegitimate child leading prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: وَلَدُ الزِّنَا، وَغَيْرُهُ سَوَاءٌ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Yunus → Al-Hasan
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Yunus, from Al-Hasan, who said: “An illegitimate child and others are equal.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ الْمُنْذِرِ الثَّوْرِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَارِثَ الْعُكْلِيَّ، عَنْ وَلَدِ الزِّنَا يَؤُمُّ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn al-Hubab → Al-Rabi‘ ibn al-Mundhir al-Thawri → Al-Harith al-‘Ukli
english.text: Zayd ibn al-Hubab narrated to us from Al-Rabi‘ ibn al-Mundhir al-Thawri, who said: I asked Al-Harith al-‘Ukli about an illegitimate child leading prayer. He said: “Yes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا هِشَامُ بْنُ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّهَا كَانَتْ إِذَا سُئِلَتْ عَنْ وَلَدِ الزِّنَا قَالَتْ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ خَطِيئَةِ أَبَوَيْهِ شَيْءٌ لَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَى

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hisham ibn ‘Urwah → His Father → ‘Aishah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Hisham ibn ‘Urwah narrated to us from his father, from ‘Aishah, that when she was asked about an illegitimate child, she would say: “He bears none of the sin of his parents; no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنَ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، قَالَ لِرَجُلٍ كَانَ يَؤُمُّ قَوْمًا بِالْعَقِيقِ لَا يُعْرَفُ مَنْ وَلَدَهُ فَنَهَاهُ أَنْ يَؤُمَّهُمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Wahhab al-Thaqafi narrated to us from Yahya ibn Sa‘id, who said: It reached me that ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz said to a man who was leading people in prayer at Al-‘Aqiq and whose father was unknown, forbidding him from leading them.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ وَلَدُ زِنَا، وَصَاحِبُ نَمِيمَةٍ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Layth → Mujahid
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Layth, from Mujahid, that he disliked for an illegitimate child or a talebearer to lead prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا دَاوُدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عَمْرُو بْنُ يَحْيَى الْمَازِنِيُّ، أَنَّ رَجُلًا حُدَّ فِي قَرْيَةٍ، فَكَانَ يَؤُمُّ أَصْحَابَهُ، فَسَأَلُوا عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، فَقَالَ: كَيْفَ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ؟ قَالُوا: قَدْ كَانَ مِنْهُ مَا كَانَ، فَأَثْنَوْا عَلَيْهِ خَيْرًا فَأَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَؤُمَّهُمْ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Dawud ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman → ‘Amr ibn Yahya al-Mazini → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Dawud ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman narrated to us, saying: ‘Amr ibn Yahya al-Mazini narrated to me that a man was punished (Hadd) in a village, and he used to lead his companions in prayer. They asked ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz, and he said: “How do you view him?” They said: “What happened happened,” and they praised him well. So he ordered him to lead them.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ الْجَوْنِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الصَّامِتِ، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ، أَنَّهُ قَدِمَ عَلَى الرَّبَذَةِ عَبْدٌ حَبَشِيٌّ، فَأُقِيمَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، فَقَالَ: تَقَدَّمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Idris → Shu‘bah → Abu ‘Imran al-Jawni → ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Samit → Abu Dharr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from Abu ‘Imran al-Jawni, from ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Samit, from Abu Dharr, that an Abyssinian slave arrived at Al-Rabadhah. The prayer was established, and he [Abu Dharr] said: “Go forward (to lead).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، أَنَّ أَبَا ذَرٍّ قَدَّمَ مَمْلُوكًا

english.narrator: Yazid → Ibn Sirin → Abu Dharr
english.text: Yazid narrated to us from Ibn Sirin, that Abu Dharr put forward a slave (to lead).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ أَنَّهُ صَلَّى خَلْفَ عَبْدٍ حَبَشِيٍّ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Ash‘ath → Ibn Sirin → Abu Dharr
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Ash‘ath, from Ibn Sirin, from Abu Dharr, that he prayed behind an Abyssinian slave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أَنَّهَا كَانَ يَؤُمُّهَا مُدَبَّرٌ لَهَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hisham ibn ‘Urwah → Abu Bakr ibn Abi Mulaykah → ‘Aishah
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Hisham ibn ‘Urwah narrated to us from Abu Bakr ibn Abi Mulaykah, from ‘Aishah, that a Mudabbar slave of hers used to lead her in prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي نَضْرَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، مَوْلَى أَبِي أُسَيْدَ، قَالَ: "تَزَوَّجْتُ وَأَنَا عَبْدٌ مَمْلُوكٌ، فَدَعَوْتُ أُنَاسًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فِيهِمْ أَبُو ذَرٍّ، وَأَبُو مَسْعُودٍ، وَأَبُو حُذَيْفَةَ، فَأُقِيمَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، فَتَقَدَّمَ أَبُو ذَرٍّ، فَقَالَ: وَرَاءَكَ، فَالْتَفَتَ إِلَى أَصْحَابِهِ، فَقَالَ: كَذَلِكَ، قَالَ: نَعَمْ، قَالَ: فَقَدَّمُونِي فَصَلَّيْتُ بِهِمْ وَأَنَا عَبْدٌ مَمْلُوكٌ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn Fudayl → Dawud ibn Abi Hind → Abu Nadrah → Abu Sa‘id
english.text: Muhammad ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Dawud ibn Abi Hind, from Abu Nadrah, from Abu Sa‘id, the freed slave of Abu Usayd, who said: “I got married while I was a slave. I invited people from the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, among them Abu Dharr, Abu Mas‘ud, and Abu Hudhayfah. The prayer was established, and Abu Dharr stepped forward. He said: ‘Behind you!’ He turned to his companions and said: ‘Is it so?’ He said: ‘Yes.’ He said: ‘So they put me forward, and I led them in prayer while I was a slave.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبَةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ الْحُصَيْنِ، عَنْ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ، "أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَؤُمُّ بَنِي عَبْدِ الْأَشْهَلِ وَهُوَ مُكَاتَبٌ، وَفِيهِمْ رِجَالٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، مِنْهُمْ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مَسْلَمَةَ، وَمَسْلَمَةُ بْنُ سَلَامَةَ، فَأَرَادُوا تَأْخِيرَهُ، فَلَمَّا سَمِعَا قِرَاءَتَهُ قَالَ: مِثْلُ هَذَا لَا يُؤَخَّرُ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi → Ibrahim ibn Abi Habibah → Dawud ibn al-Husayn → Abu Sufyan
english.text: ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Ibrahim ibn Abi Habibah, from Dawud ibn al-Husayn, from Abu Sufyan, that he used to lead Banu ‘Abd al-Ashhal in prayer while he was a Mukatab (slave contracting for freedom), and among them were men from the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, including Muhammad ibn Maslamah and Maslamah ibn Salamah. They wanted to move him back, but when they heard his recitation, they said: “Someone like this should not be moved back.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ قَالَا: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ الْعَبْدُ

english.narrator: ‘Abd Allah ibn Idris → Hisham → Al-Hasan/Ibn Sirin
english.text: ‘Abd Allah ibn Idris narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan and Ibn Sirin, who both said: “There is no harm in a slave leading prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يَؤُمَّ الْعَبْدُ

english.narrator: Ibn Fudayl → Al-Hasan ibn ‘Ubayd Allah → Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us from Al-Hasan ibn ‘Ubayd Allah, from Ibrahim, that he saw no harm in a slave leading prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ بَيَانٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ الْعَبْدُ

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Sa‘id → Sufyan → Bayan → ‘Amir
english.text: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us from Sufyan, from Bayan, from ‘Amir, who said: “There is no harm in a slave leading prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ شَهْرٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَؤُمَّ الْعَبْدُ، إِذَا كَانَ أَفْقَهَهُمْ

english.narrator: Ibn Idris → Layth → Shahr
english.text: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Layth, from Shahr, who said: “There is no harm in a slave leading prayer if he is the most knowledgeable of them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ زِيَادٍ، مَوْلَى أُمِّ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: صَلَّى خَلْفِي سَالِمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ وَأَنَا عَبْدٌ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Ziyad
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Ziyad, the freed slave of Umm al-Hasan, who said: “Salim ibn ‘Abd Allah prayed behind me while I was a slave.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ كَهَمْسٍ، عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ الْجُرَيْرِيِّ، أَنَّ أَبَا مِجْلَزٍ كَرِهَ إِمَامَةَ لْعَبْدِ وَأَنَّ الْحَسَنَ لَمْ يَرَ بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir → Kahmas → Al-‘Abbas al-Jurayri → Abu Mijlaz
english.text: Mu‘tamir narrated to us from Kahmas, from Al-‘Abbas al-Jurayri, that Abu Mijlaz disliked the Imamate of a slave, while Al-Hasan saw no harm in it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْحُ بْنُ عُبَادَةَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ، أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَأْتُونَ عَائِشَةَ وَأَبُوهُ وَعُبَيْدُ بْنُ عُمَيْرٍ، وَالْمِسْوَرُ بْنُ مَخْرَمَةَ، وَأُنَاسٌ كَثِيرٌ، فَيَؤُمُّهُمْ أَبُو عَمْرٍو، مَوْلًى لِعَائِشَةَ وَأَبُو عَمْرٍو، حِينَئِذٍ غُلَامٌ لَمْ يُعْتَقْ

english.narrator: Rawh ibn ‘Ubadah → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Ubayd Allah ibn Abi Mulaykah → Abu ‘Amr
english.text: Rawh ibn ‘Ubadah narrated to us, saying: Ibn Jurayj informed me from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Ubayd Allah ibn Abi Mulaykah, that they used to come to ‘Aishah—including his father, ‘Ubayd ibn ‘Umayr, Al-Miswar ibn Makhramah, and many people—and Abu ‘Amr, a freed slave of ‘Aishah, would lead them in prayer. Abu ‘Amr at that time was a boy who had not been freed.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا رَوْحُ بْنُ عُبَادَةَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَؤُمُّنَا فِي مَسْجِدِنَا هَذَا عَبْدٌ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً مَسْجِدٌ كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ شُرَيْحٌ

english.narrator: Rawh ibn ‘Ubadah → Shu‘bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: Rawh ibn ‘Ubadah narrated to us from Shu‘bah, from Al-Hakam, who said: “A slave used to lead us in prayer in this mosque of ours for forty years”—a mosque where Shurayh used to pray.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ بَشَّارِ بْنِ كِدَامٍ السُّلَمِيِّ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ أَنَّهُ صَلَّى خَلْفَ مَمْلُوكٍ فِي حَائِطٍ مِنْ حِيطَانِهِ، وَنَاسٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → Bashar ibn Kidam al-Sulami → ‘Amr ibn Maysarah → Al-Hasan ibn ‘Ali
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Bashar ibn Kidam al-Sulami, from ‘Amr ibn Maysarah, from Al-Hasan ibn ‘Ali, that he prayed behind a slave in one of his gardens, along with people from his family.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بَشِيرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ بِسْطَامٍ التَّمِيمِيِّ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، قَالَ: لَا يَؤُمُّ الْمَمْلُوكُ وَفِيهِمْ حُرٌّ، وَلَا يَؤُمُّ مَنْ لَمْ يَحُجَّ وَفِيهِمْ مَنْ قَدْ حَجَّ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Bashir ibn Sulayman → Yahya ibn Bistam al-Tamimi → Al-Dahhak
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Bashir ibn Sulayman narrated to us from Yahya ibn Bistam al-Tamimi, from Al-Dahhak, who said: “A slave should not lead prayer while there is a free man among them, nor should one who has not performed Hajj lead prayer while there is one who has performed Hajj among them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ أَبِي حَبِيبَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: خَرَجْنَا مَعَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ، وَحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، وَابْنِ أَبِي أَحْمَرَ، إِلَى يَنْبُعَ، فَحَضَرَتِ الصَّلَاةُ فَقَدَّمُونِي فَصَلَّيْتُ بِهِمْ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn al-Hubab → Ibrahim ibn Abi Habibah → ‘Abd Allah ibn Abi Sufyan → His Father
english.text: Zayd ibn al-Hubab narrated to us, saying: Ibrahim ibn Abi Habibah narrated to me, saying: ‘Abd Allah ibn Abi Sufyan narrated to me from his father, who said: “We went out with ‘Abd Allah ibn Ja‘far, Husayn ibn ‘Ali, and Ibn Abi Ahmar to Yanbu‘. The prayer time arrived, so they put me forward, and I led them in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ الْغَسِيلِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي الْمُنْذِرُ بْنُ أَبِي أُسَيْدَ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَبِي يُصَلِّي خَلْفِي، فَرُبَّمَا قَالَ لِي: يَا بُنَيَّ، طَوَّلْتَ بِنَا الْيَوْمَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sulayman ibn al-Ghasil → Al-Mundhir ibn Abi Usayd al-Ansari → His Father
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sulayman ibn al-Ghasil narrated to us, saying: Al-Mundhir ibn Abi Usayd al-Ansari narrated to me, saying: My father used to pray behind me, and perhaps he would say to me: “O my son, you prolonged it for us today.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ الْمَكِّيُّ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: لَا يَؤُمُّ الرَّجُلُ أَبَاهُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Ibrahim ibn Yazid al-Makki → ‘Ata’
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Ibrahim ibn Yazid al-Makki informed us from ‘Ata’, who said: “A man should not lead his father in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبَانُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ الْعَطَّارُ، عَنْ بُدَيْلِ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ الْعُقَيْلِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي عَطِيَّةَ، رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ، قَالَ: كَانَ مَالِكُ بْنُ الْحُوَيْرِثِ، يَأْتِينَا فِي مُصَلَّانَا هَذَا نَتَحَدَّثُ، فَحَضَرَتِ الصَّلَاةُ فَقُلْنَا لَهُ: تَقَدَّمْ، فَقَالَ: لَا، يَتَقَدَّمُ بَعْضُكُمْ حَتَّى أُحَدِّثُكُمْ لِمَ لَا أَتَقَدَّمُ، سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ: مَنْ زَارَ قَوْمًا فَلَا يَؤُمُّهُمْ، وَلْيَؤُمَّهُمْ رَجُلٌ مِنْهُمْ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Aban ibn Yazid al-‘Attar → Budayl ibn Maysarah al-‘Uqayli → Abu ‘Atiyyah → Malik ibn al-Huwayrith
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Aban ibn Yazid al-‘Attar narrated to us from Budayl ibn Maysarah al-‘Uqayli, from Abu ‘Atiyyah—a man from them—who said: Malik ibn al-Huwayrith used to come to this prayer place of ours, and we would talk. The prayer time arrived, so we said to him: “Step forward.” He said: “No, let one of you step forward so I can tell you why I do not step forward. I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: ‘Whoever visits a people should not lead them in prayer; rather, a man from among them should lead them.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، وَهُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ سِمَاكِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ الضَّبِّيِّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، وَابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ وَهُمَا مُتَرَبِّعَانِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir/Hushaym → Mughirah → Simak ibn Salamah al-Dabbi → Ibn ‘Umar/Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir and Hushaym narrated to us from Mughirah, from Simak ibn Salamah al-Dabbi, who said: “I saw Ibn ‘Umar and Ibn ‘Abbas sitting cross-legged in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ عُقْبَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسًا يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → ‘Uqbah
english.text: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from ‘Uqbah, who said: “I saw Anas praying sitting cross-legged.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدٍ الطَّائِيِّ، عَنْ أَخِيهِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسًا يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sa‘īd ibn ‘Ubayd al-Ṭā’ī → His Brother
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sa‘īd ibn ‘Ubayd al-Ṭā’ī, from his brother, who said: “I saw Anas praying sitting cross-legged.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عُمَرَ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسًا، يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا عَلَى طُنْفُسَةٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → ‘Umar al-Anṣārī
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from ‘Umar al-Anṣārī, who said: “I saw Anas praying sitting cross-legged on a Ṭunfusah (small carpet).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ جُحَادَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَالِمًا، يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا، وَمُتَّكِئًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Muḥammad ibn Juḥādah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Muḥammad ibn Juḥādah, who said: “I saw Sālim praying sitting cross-legged and leaning.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ رَجَاءٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Manṣūr → Rajā’ → Mujāhid
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Manṣūr, from Rajā’, from Mujāhid, who said: “He prays sitting cross-legged.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا، وَمُتَّكِئًا

english.narrator: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh → Ḥumayd
english.text: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh narrated to us, from Ḥumayd, who said: “I saw Abū Bakr praying sitting cross-legged and leaning.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَطَاءً يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Abd al-Malik
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Abd al-Malik, who said: “I saw ‘Aṭā’ praying sitting cross-legged.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا جَرِيرُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ سِيرِينَ يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Jarīr ibn Ḥāzim
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Jarīr ibn Ḥāzim narrated to us, saying: “I saw Ibn Sīrīn praying sitting cross-legged.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَجْلِسُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn Mūsā → Isrā’īl → Jābir → Abū Ja‘far
english.text: ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn Mūsā narrated to us, from Isrā’īl, from Jābir, from Abū Ja‘far, that he used to sit cross-legged in prayer.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ دَلْهَمٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ فِي التَّطَوُّعِ مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Al-Faḍl ibn Dalham → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Al-Faḍl ibn Dalham, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “There is no harm in praying voluntary prayer sitting cross-legged.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنِ الْهَيْثَمِ بْنِ شِهَابٍ، أَنَّهُ رَأَى رَجُلًا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي قَاعِدًا مُتَرَبِّعًا، فَنَهَاهُ، فَأَبَى أَنْ يُطِيعَهُ، فَقَالَ الْهَيْثَمُ: سَمِعْتُ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ مَسْعُودٍ يَقُولُ: لَأَنْ أَقْعُدَ عَلَى رَضْفَتَيْنِ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ أَنْ أَقْعُدَ مُتَرَبِّعًا فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl → Ḥuṣayn → Al-Haytham ibn Shihāb
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Ḥuṣayn, from Al-Haytham ibn Shihāb, that he saw a man from his people praying sitting cross-legged. He forbade him, but the man refused to obey. Al-Haytham said: I heard ‘Abd Allāh ibn Mas‘ūd saying: “To sit on two hot stones is dearer to me than to sit cross-legged in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، عَنْ التَّرَبُّعِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ، فَكَأَنَّهُ كَرِهَهُ قَالَ: وَأَحْسِبُهُ قَالَ: كَرِهَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Shu‘bah narrated to us, saying: “I asked Al-Ḥakam about sitting cross-legged in prayer, and he seemed to dislike it.” He said: And I think he said: “Ibn ‘Abbās disliked it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ مُتَرَبِّعًا، وَقَالَ: اجْلِسْ غَيْرَ جِلْسَتِكَ لِلْحَدِيثِ

english.narrator: Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar → Ḥajjāj → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, that he disliked praying sitting cross-legged, and said: “Sit differently from your sitting for conversation.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يَجْلِسَ فِي الصَّلَاةِ جِلْسَةَ الرَّجُلِ يُحَدِّثُ أَصْحَابَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Sufyān → Al-Zubayr ibn ‘Adī → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Al-Zubayr ibn ‘Adī, from Ibrāhīm, that he disliked sitting in prayer in the posture of a man talking to his companions.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ، عَنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ الصَّنْعَانِيِّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ مُتَرَبِّعًا فِي آخَرِ صَلَاتِهِ، حِينَ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ مِنَ السَّجْدَةِ الْأَخِيرَةِ، فَلَمَّا صَلَّى قُلْتُ لَهُ، فَقَالَ: إِنِّي أَشْتَكِي رِجْلِي

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Jarīr ibn Ḥāzim → Al-Mughīrah ibn Ḥakīm al-Ṣan‘ānī → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarīr ibn Ḥāzim narrated to us, from Al-Mughīrah ibn Ḥakīm al-Ṣan‘ānī, who said: I saw Ibn ‘Umar sitting cross-legged at the end of his prayer, when he raised his head from the last prostration. When he finished praying, I mentioned it to him, and he said: “I am suffering from my leg.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، أَنَّ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، صَلَّى مُتَرَبِّعًا مِنْ وَجَعٍ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Nāfi‘, that Ibn ‘Umar prayed sitting cross-legged due to pain.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ يُكْرَهُ أَنْ يَتَرَبَّعُ الرَّجُلُ فِي صَلَاتِهِ حَتَّى يَتَشَهَّدَ

english.narrator: Al-Thaqafī → Ayyūb → Muḥammad
english.text: Al-Thaqafī narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Muḥammad, who said: “It was disliked for a man to sit cross-legged in his prayer until he recites the Tashahhud.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: نُبِّئْتُ أَنَّ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، صَلَّى مُتَرَبِّعًا وَقَالَ: إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ بِسُنَّةٍ، إِنَّمَا أَفْعَلُهُ مِنْ وَجَعٍ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → Ibn Sīrīn → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: I was informed that Ibn ‘Umar prayed sitting cross-legged and said: “It is not Sunnah; I only do it because of pain.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ "أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ التَّرَبُّعَ وَقَالَ: جِلْسَةُ مَمْلَكَةٍ

english.narrator: Ibn Idrīs → Layth → Ṭāwūs
english.text: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Layth, from Ṭāwūs, that he disliked sitting cross-legged and said: “It is the sitting posture of kingship.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا صَلَّى قَاعِدًا جَعَلَ قِيَامَهُ مُتَرَبِّعًا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “If he prays sitting, he makes his standing [position] cross-legged.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بُزَيْعٍ، قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى سَالِمٍ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي جَالِسًا جَعَلَ قِيَامَهُ مُتَرَبِّعًا، فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَرْكَعَ رَكَعَ وَهُوَ مُتَرَبِّعٌ، وَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَسْجُدَ ثَنَى رِجْلَهُ

english.narrator: Asbāṭ ibn Muḥammad → Muṭarrif → Sulaymān ibn Buzay‘ → Sālim
english.text: Asbāṭ ibn Muḥammad narrated to us, from Muṭarrif, from Sulaymān ibn Buzay‘, who said: “I entered upon Sālim while he was praying sitting. He made his standing [position] cross-legged. When he wanted to bow, he bowed while cross-legged, and when he wanted to prostrate, he folded his leg.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: كَانَ سُفْيَانُ إِذَا صَلَّى جَالِسًا قِيَامَهُ مُتَرَبِّعًا، فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَرْكَعَ رَكَعَ وَهُوَ مُتَرَبِّعٌ، فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَسْجُدَ ثَنَى رِجْلَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: “When Sufyān prayed sitting, his standing [position] was cross-legged. When he wanted to bow, he bowed while cross-legged, and when he wanted to prostrate, he folded his leg.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: "إِذَا صَلَّى مُتَرَبِّعًا، قَالَ مِسْعَرٌ: أَوْ كَمَا قَالَ: يَجْلِسُ، فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَرْكَعَ، أَوْ يَسْجُدَ ثَنَى رِجْلَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mis‘ar → Ḥammād → Mujāhid → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Mis‘ar narrated to us, from Ḥammād, from Mujāhid, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: “If he prays cross-legged - Mis‘ar said: or as he said: he sits - when he wants to bow or prostrate, he folds his leg.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا، فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَرْكَعَ ثَنَى رِجْلَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Hishām → Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Hishām, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: “He prays cross-legged, and when he wants to bow, he folds his leg.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنٌ، عَنْ أَبِي حَفْصٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَنَسًا يُصَلِّي مُتَرَبِّعًا، فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَرْكَعَ ثَنَى رِجْلَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ḥasan → Abū Ḥafṣ → Anas
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Ḥasan narrated to us, from Abū Ḥafṣ, who said: “I saw Anas praying cross-legged, and when he wanted to bow, he folded his leg.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَدْخُلُ الْمَسْجِدَ وَقَدْ تَمَّ الصَّفُّ قَالَ: إِنِ اسْتَطَاعَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ فِي الصَّفِّ دَخَلَ، وَإِلَّا أَخَذَ بِيَدِ رَجُلٍ فَأَقَامَهُ مَعَهُ، وَلَمْ يَقُمْ وَحْدَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām → ‘Abd al-Malik → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-Malik, from ‘Aṭā’, regarding a man who enters the mosque when the row is full. He said: “If he can enter the row, he enters. Otherwise, he takes a man by the hand and makes him stand with him, and does not stand alone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَجِيءُ إِلَى الصَّفِّ وَقَدِ امْتَلَأَ؟ قَالَ: مُرْ رَجُلًا فَأَقِمْهُ مَعَكَ، فَإِنْ صَلَّيْتَ وَحْدَكَ فَأَعِدْ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → ‘Amr ibn Maymūn → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth narrated to us, from ‘Amr ibn Maymūn, who said: I said to Ibrāhīm: “I come to the row and it is full?” He said: “Order a man to stand with you. If you pray alone, repeat [the prayer].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، وَعَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَؤُمُّ نِسَاءَهُ فِي الْمَكْتُوبَةِ، لَيْسَ مَعَهُنَّ رَجُلٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Mu‘āwiyah and ‘Abdah ibn Sulaymān → Hishām → His Father
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Mu‘āwiyah and ‘Abdah ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Hishām, from his father, that he used to lead his women in the obligatory prayer when there was no man with them.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: جَعَلَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابَ لِلنَّاسِ قَارِئِينَ فِي رَمَضَانَ، فَكَانَ أَبِي يُصَلِّي بِالنَّاسِ، وَابْنُ أَبِي حَثْمَةَ يُصَلِّي بِالنِّسَاءِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Hishām → His Father
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Hishām narrated to us, from his father, who said: “‘Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb appointed reciters for the people in Ramadan. My father used to lead the men in prayer, and Ibn Abī Ḥathmah used to lead the women.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ غَالِبٍ أَبِي الْهُذَيْلِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ أُصَلِّي فِي الْحَيِّ فِي زَمَنِ الْحَجَّاجِ، وَمَا خَلْفِي إِلَّا امْرَأَةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Ghālib Abū al-Hudhayl → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Ghālib Abū al-Hudhayl, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “I used to lead prayer in the neighborhood during the time of Al-Ḥajjāj, and there was no one behind me except a woman.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الشَّعْبِيَّ، وَعَطَاءً، عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَؤُمُّ النِّسَاءَ لَيْسَ مَعَهُنَّ رَجُلٌ؟ فَقَالَا: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Jābir → Al-Sha‘bī and ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Jābir, who said: I asked Al-Sha‘bī and ‘Aṭā’ about a man leading women in prayer when there is no man with them. They said: “There is no harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الثَّقَفِيِّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَرْفَجَةُ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَلِيٌّ، يَأْمُرُ النَّاسَ بِقِيَامِ رَمَضَانَ، وَكَانَ يَجْعَلُ لِلرِّجَالِ إِمَامًا، وَلِلنِّسَاءِ إِمَامًا قَالَ عَرْفَجَةُ: فَأَمَرَنِي عَلِيٌّ، فَكُنْتُ إِمَامَ النِّسَاءِ

english.narrator: Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd Allāh al-Thaqafī → ‘Arfajah → ‘Alī
english.text: Marwān ibn Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd Allāh al-Thaqafī, who said: ‘Arfajah narrated to us, saying: “‘Alī used to order people to perform Qiyām in Ramadan, and he would appoint an Imam for the men and an Imam for the women.” ‘Arfajah said: “‘Alī ordered me, so I was the Imam of the women.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَؤُمُّ النِّسْوَةَ فِي رَمَضَانَ، قَالَ: كَانَ لَا يَرَى بِهِ بَأْسًا، إِذَا كَانَ الرَّجُلُ لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ قَالَ: وَإِنْ كَانَ الرَّجُلُ لَيَخْرُجُ فَتَفُوتُهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ، فَيَرْجِعُ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ فَيَجْمَعُهُمْ، فَيُصَلِّي بِهِمْ

english.narrator: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām narrated to us, from Hishām, from Al-Ḥasan, who was asked about a man leading women in prayer in Ramadan. He said: “He saw no harm in it, provided the man is trustworthy.” He said: “And if a man goes out and misses the congregational prayer, he returns to his family, gathers them, and leads them in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ مُطَهَّرِ بْنِ جُوَيْرِيَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا مِجْلَزٍ، وَلَهُ مَسْجِدٌ فِي دَارِهِ، فَرُبَّمَا جَمَعَ بِأَهْلِهِ وَغِلْمَانِهِ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb → Muṭahhar ibn Juwayriyah → Abū Mijlaz
english.text: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb narrated to us, from Muṭahhar ibn Juwayriyah, who said: “I saw Abū Mijlaz, and he had a mosque in his house. Sometimes he would lead his family and servants in prayer.”
