arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ نُعَيْمٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: إِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ الْإِمَامِ طَرِيقٌ، أَوْ نَهْرٌ، أَوْ حَائِطٌ، فَلَيْسَ مَعَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → Layth → Nu‘aym → ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth narrated to us, from Layth, from Nu‘aym, who said: ‘Umar said: “If there is a road, river, or wall between him and the Imam, then he is not with him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بِصَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ، إِذَا كَانَ بَيْنَهُمَا طَرِيقٌ، أَوْ نِسَاءٌ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdī → Sufyān → Al-A‘mash → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrāhīm, that he disliked following the Imam in prayer if there was a road or women between them.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ أَبِي عَزَّةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ الْمَرْأَةِ تَأْتَمُّ بِالْإِمَامِ وَبَيْنَهُمَا طَرِيقٌ، فَقَالَ: لَيْسَ ذَلِكَ لَهَا

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdī → Isrā’īl → ‘Īsā ibn Abī ‘Azzah → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Isrā’īl, from ‘Īsā ibn Abī ‘Azzah, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: I asked him about a woman following the Imam while there is a road between them. He said: “That is not permissible for her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَنَسُ يَجْمَعُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ وَهُوَ فِي دَارِ نَافِعِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْحَارِثِ، بَيْتٌ مُشْرِفٌ عَلَى الْمَسْجِدِ لَهُ بَابٌ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ، فَكَانَ يَجْمَعُ فِيهِ، وَيَأْتَمُّ بِالْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Hushaym → Ḥumayd → Anas
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, from Ḥumayd, who said: “Anas used to pray Friday prayer with the Imam while he was in the house of Nāfi‘ ibn ‘Abd al-Ḥārith, a house overlooking the mosque with a door to the mosque. He would pray Friday prayer there and follow the Imam.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنْ صَالِحٍ، مَوْلَى التَّوْأَمَةِ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ فَوْقَ الْمَسْجِدِ بِصَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ وَهُوَ أَسْفَلُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ibn Abī Dhi’b → Ṣāliḥ (Mawla Al-Taw'amah) → Abū Hurayrah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Dhi’b, from Ṣāliḥ, the freed slave of Al-Taw’amah, who said: “I prayed with Abū Hurayrah on top of the mosque, following the Imam’s prayer while he was below.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مِجْلَزٍ، فِي الْمَرْأَةِ تُصَلِّي وَبَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَ الْإِمَامِ حَائِطٌ، قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَتْ تَسْمَعُ التَّكْبِيرَ أَجْزَأَهَا ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir → Layth → Abū Mijlaz
english.text: Mu‘tamir narrated to us, from Layth, from Abū Mijlaz, regarding a woman praying with a wall between her and the Imam. He said: “If she can hear the Takbīr, that suffices her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَامِرٍ الْعَقَدِيُّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَالِمَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى فَوْقَ ظَهْرِ الْمَسْجِدِ صَلَاةَ الْمَغْرِبِ، وَمَعَهُ رَجُلٌ آخَرَ يَعْنِي وَيَأْتَمُّ بِالْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Abū ‘Āmir al-‘Aqadī → Sa‘īd ibn Muslim → Sālim ibn ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Abū ‘Āmir al-‘Aqadī narrated to us, from Sa‘īd ibn Muslim, who said: “I saw Sālim ibn ‘Abd Allāh pray Maghrib on the roof of the mosque, along with another man,” meaning he was following the Imam.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ إِلَى جَنْبِ مَسْجِدِنَا سَطْحٌ، عَنْ يَمِينِ الْمَسْجِدِ أَسْفَلُ مِنَ الْإِمَامِ، فَكَانَ قَوْمٌ هَارِبِينَ فِي إِمَارَةِ الْحَجَّاجِ، وَبَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ الْمَسْجِدِ حَائِطٌ طَوِيلٌ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ السَّطْحِ، وَيَأْتَمُّونَ بِالْإِمَامِ فَذَكَرْتُهُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ فَرَآهُ حَسَنًا

english.narrator: Jarīr → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Manṣūr, who said: “There was a roof next to our mosque, to the right of the mosque, lower than the Imam. Some people who were in hiding during the rule of Al-Ḥajjāj used to pray on that roof while there was a long wall between them and the mosque, following the Imam. I mentioned this to Ibrāhīm, and he considered it good.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ مُحَمَّدٌ عَنْ الرَّجُلِ يَكُونُ عَلَى ظَهْرِ بَيْتٍ، يُصَلِّي بِصَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ فِي رَمَضَانَ؟ فَقَالَ: لَا أَعْلَمُ بِهِ بَأْسًا، إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ الْإِمَامِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī → Ibn ‘Awn → Muḥammad
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Abī ‘Adī narrated to us, from Ibn ‘Awn, who said: Muḥammad was asked about a man on the roof of a house praying with the Imam’s prayer in Ramadan. He said: “I do not know any harm in it, unless he is in front of the Imam.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، أَنَّ عُرْوَةَ كَانَ يُصَلِّي بِصَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ وَهُوَ فِي دَارِ حُمَيْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، وَبَيْنَهُمَا وَبَيْنَ الْمَسْجِدِ طَرِيقٌ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah → ‘Urwah
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Mahdī narrated to us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, that ‘Urwah used to pray following the Imam’s prayer while he was in the house of Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Ḥārith, and there was a road between them and the mosque.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ عَنْ الْمُؤَذِّنِ يُقِيمُ فِي الْمِئْذَنَةِ، وَيُصَلِّي بِصَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ؟ قَالَ: يُجْزِيهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Aṭā’, who was asked about the Mu'adhdhin making the Iqamah in the minaret and praying following the Imam’s prayer. He said: “It suffices him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ صَلَاةِ الْمُؤَذِّنِينَ فَوْقَ الْمَسْجِدِ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ، بِصَلَاةِ الْإِمَامِ وَهُوَ أَسْفَلُ؟ قَالَ: يُجْزِيهِمْ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abū Ḥanīfah → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Abū Ḥanīfah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: I asked him about the prayer of the Mu'adhdhins on top of the mosque on Friday, following the Imam’s prayer while he is below. He said: “It suffices them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ الْمُؤَذِّنِ يُصَلِّي فِي صَوْمَعَتِهِ، وَيَأْتَمُّ بِالْإِمَامِ؟ فَكَرِهَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: I asked him about the Mu'adhdhin praying in his minaret and following the Imam. He disliked that.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي ثَلَاثَةِ أَثْوَابٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Sulaymān al-Taymī → Ibn Sīrīn → Abū Hurayrah → ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Sulaymān al-Taymī, from Ibn Sīrīn, from Abū Hurayrah, who said: ‘Umar said: “A woman prays in three garments.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ فِي كَمْ تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ؟ فَقَالَتْ: ائْتِ عَلِيًّا فَاسْأَلْهُ، ثُمَّ ارْجِعْ إِلَيَّ فَأَتَى عَلِيًّا فَسَأَلَهُ، فَقَالَ: فِي دِرْعٍ سَابِغٍ وَخِمَارٍ فَرَجَعَ إِلَيْهَا فَأَخْبَرَهَا، فَقَالَتْ: صَدَقَ

english.narrator: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus → Al-Awzā‘ī → Makḥūl → ‘Ā’ishah / ‘Alī
english.text: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus narrated to us, from Al-Awzā‘ī, from Makḥūl, who said: I asked ‘Ā’ishah: “In how many [garments] does a woman pray?” She said: “Go to ‘Alī and ask him, then return to me.” So he went to ‘Alī and asked him. He said: “In a long chemise (Dir‘) and a veil (Khimār).” He returned to her and informed her, and she said: “He spoke the truth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ بُكَيْرٍ، عَنِ الْأَشَجِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْخَوْلَانِيِّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ مَيْمُونَةَ زَوْجَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، تُصَلِّي فِي دِرْعٍ وَاحِدٍ فَضْلًا، وَقَدْ وَضَعَتْ بَعْضَ كُمِّهَا عَلَى رَأْسِهَا قَالَ: وَكَانَ عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ يَتِيمًا فِي حِجْرِهَا

english.narrator: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām → Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq → Bukayr → Al-Ashajj → ‘Abd Allāh al-Khawlānī → Maymūnah / ‘Ubayd Allāh
english.text: ‘Abbād ibn al-‘Awwām narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq, from Bukayr, from Al-Ashajj, from ‘Abd Allāh al-Khawlānī, who said: “I saw Maymūnah, the wife of the Prophet ﷺ, praying in a single spacious chemise, having placed part of her sleeve over her head.” He said: And ‘Ubayd Allāh was an orphan under her care.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ بُكَيْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْأَشَجِّ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ الْخَوْلَانِيِّ، عَنْ مَيْمُونَةَ بِنْتِ الْحَارِثِ، زَوْجِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنَّهَا صَلَّتْ فِي دِرْعٍ وَخِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mālik ibn Anas → Bukayr ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Ashajj → ‘Ubayd Allāh al-Khawlānī → Maymūnah bint al-Ḥārith
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Mālik ibn Anas narrated to us, from Bukayr ibn ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Ashajj, from ‘Ubayd Allāh al-Khawlānī, from Maymūnah bint al-Ḥārith, the wife of the Prophet ﷺ, that she prayed in a chemise and a veil.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَتْنِي أُمِّي، أَنَّهَا سَأَلَتْ أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ زَوْجَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فِي أَيِّ شَيْءٍ تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ؟ فَقَالَتْ: تُصَلِّي فِي دِرْعٍ سَابِغٍ يُغَطِّي قَدَمَيْهَا وَالْخِمَارِ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → Muḥammad ibn Zayd → His Mother → Umm Salamah
english.text: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Zayd, who said: My mother narrated to me that she asked Umm Salamah, the wife of the Prophet ﷺ: “In what does a woman pray?” She said: “She prays in a long chemise that covers her feet and a veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ مُهَاجِرِ بْنِ قُنْفُذٍ، عَنْ أُمِّهِ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَتْ: تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي الدِّرْعِ السَّابِغِ وَالْخِمَارِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Hishām ibn Sa‘d → Muḥammad ibn Zayd → Muhājir ibn Qunfudh → His Mother → Umm Salamah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Hishām ibn Sa‘d informed us, from Muḥammad ibn Zayd, from Muhājir ibn Qunfudh, from his mother, from Umm Salamah, who said: “A woman prays in a long chemise and a veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أُمِّ ثَوْرٍ، عَنْ زَوْجِهَا بِشْرٍ، أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ فِي كَمْ تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ؟ فَقَالَ: فِي دِرْعٍ وَخِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Jābir → Umm Thawr → Her Husband Bishr → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Jābir, from Umm Thawr, from her husband Bishr, that he asked Ibn ‘Abbās: “In how many [garments] does a woman pray?” He said: “In a chemise and a veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: إِذَا صَلَّتِ الْمَرْأَةُ، فَلْتُصَلِّ فِي ثِيَابِهَا كُلِّهَا الدِّرْعُ وَالْخِمَارُ وَالْمِلْحَفَةُ

english.narrator: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Numayr → ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Numayr narrated to us, from ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: “When a woman prays, she should pray in all her clothes: the chemise, the veil, and the wrap (Milḥafah).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَبِيدَةُ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي الدِّرْعِ وَالْخِمَارِ وَالْحَقْوِ قَالَ أَشْعَثُ: عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، مِثْلَهُ، فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: مَا هَذِهِ الْخُمُرُ؟ فَقَالَ: "الْخُمُرُ مَا خَمَّرَ، وَكَانَتْ الْأَنْصَارُ تُسَمِّي الْإِزَارَ: الْحَقْوَ

english.narrator: Abū Khālid → Ash‘ath → Muḥammad → ‘Abīdah
english.text: Abū Khālid narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Muḥammad, from ‘Abīdah, who said: “A woman prays in a chemise, a veil, and a waist wrapper (Ḥaqw).” Ash‘ath said: From Muḥammad, similar to it. I said to him: “What are these veils (Khumur)?” He said: “The Khumur are what cover. The Ansar used to call the Izār: Ḥaqw.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي ثَلَاثَةِ أَثْوَابٍ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: “A woman prays in three garments.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو هِلَالٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَسْتَحِبُّ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ الْمَرْأَةُ فِي ثَلَاثَةِ أَثْوَابٍ، فِي الدِّرْعِ وَالْخِمَارِ وَالْحَقْوِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abū Hilāl → Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Abū Hilāl narrated to us, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: “It was recommended for a woman to pray in three garments: the chemise, the veil, and the waist wrapper.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُرَخِّصُ لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ فِي الدِّرْعِ وَالْجِلْبَابِ

english.narrator: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, that he used to permit a woman to pray in a chemise and a Jilbāb.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَتِ امْرَأَةٌ لِأَبِي: إِنِّي امْرَأَةٌ حُبْلَى، وَإِنَّهُ يَشُقُّ عَلَيَّ أَنْ أُصَلِّيَ فِي الْمُنْطَقِ، أَفَأُصَلِّي فِي دِرْعٍ وَخِمَارٍ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām ibn ‘Urwah → His Father
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām ibn ‘Urwah, who said: A woman said to my father: “I am a pregnant woman, and it is difficult for me to pray with a waistband. Can I pray in a chemise and a veil?” He said: “Yes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ الْجُرَيْرِيِّ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي دِرْعٍ وَخِمَارٍ صَفِيقٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū Usāmah → Al-Jurayrī → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Al-Jurayrī, from ‘Ikrimah, who said: “A woman prays in a chemise and a thick veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ هَمَّامٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي دِرْعٍ صَفِيقٍ، وَخِمَارٍ صَفِيقٍ

english.narrator: Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Hammām → Qatādah → Jābir ibn Zayd
english.text: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, from Hammām, from Qatādah, from Jābir ibn Zayd, who said: “A woman prays in a thick chemise and a thick veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَطَاءٌ: تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي دِرْعٍ وَخِمَارٍ

english.narrator: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus → Al-Awzā‘ī → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus narrated to us, from Al-Awzā‘ī, who said: ‘Aṭā’ said: “A woman prays in a chemise and a veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، فَقَالَ: فِي دِرْعٍ وَخِمَارٍ وَسَأَلْتُ حَمَّادًا، فَقَالَ: تُصَلِّي فِي دِرْعٍ، وَمِلْحَفَةٍ تُغَطِّي رَأْسَهَا

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam / Ḥammād
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, who said: I asked Al-Ḥakam, and he said: “In a chemise and a veil.” And I asked Ḥammād, and he said: “She prays in a chemise and a wrap that covers her head.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: أَلَا لَا تُصَلِّي الْمَرْأَةُ فِي أَقَلَّ مِنْ أَرْبَعَةِ أَثْوَابٍ

english.narrator: Ibn Fuḍayl → Layth → Mujāhid
english.text: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Layth, from Mujāhid, who said: “Verily, a woman should not pray in less than four garments.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ مُعَاذَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أَنَّهَا قَامَتْ تُصَلِّي فِي دِرْعٍ وَخِمَارٍ، فَأَتَتْهَا الْأَمَةُ فَأَلْقَتْ عَلَيْهَا ثَوْبًا

english.narrator: Ibn Fuḍayl → ‘Āṣim → Mu‘ādhah → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from ‘Āṣim, from Mu‘ādhah, from ‘Ā’ishah, that she stood up to pray in a chemise and a veil, so the slave girl came to her and threw a garment over her.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَزْهَرُ السَّمَّانُ، عَنْ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَ: تَتَّزِرُ بِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Azhar al-Sammān → Ibn ‘Awn → Muḥammad
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Azhar al-Sammān narrated to us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Muḥammad, who said: “She wraps herself with it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ ذَرٍّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ مُجَاهِدًا، وَعَطَاءً عَنْ الْمَرْأَةِ تَحْضُرُهَا الصَّلَاةُ وَلَيْسَ لَهَا إِلَّا ثَوْبٌ وَاحِدٌ؟ قَالَ: تَتَّزِرُ بِهِ

english.narrator: Ibn Fuḍayl → ‘Umar ibn Dharr → Mujāhid and ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from ‘Umar ibn Dharr, who said: I asked Mujāhid and ‘Aṭā’ about a woman for whom the prayer time comes and she has only one garment. He said: “She wraps herself with it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ ذَرٍّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً عَنْ الْمَرْأَةِ لَا يَكُونُ لَهَا إِلَّا ثَوْبٌ وَاحِدٌ؟ قَالَ: تَتَّزِرُ بِهِ قَالَ وَكِيعٌ: يَعْنِي إِذَا كَانَ صَغِيرًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → ‘Umar ibn Dharr → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: ‘Umar ibn Dharr narrated to us, saying: I asked ‘Aṭā’ about a woman who has only one garment. He said: “She wraps herself with it.” Wakī‘ said: Meaning if it is small.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِكْرِمَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ أَنَّهُ أَمَّهُمْ فِي قَمِيصٍ وَاحِدٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Wakī‘ → ‘Ikrimah ibn ‘Ammār → ‘Aṭā’ → Jābir
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: ‘Ikrimah ibn ‘Ammār narrated to us, from ‘Aṭā’, from Jābir, that he led them in prayer in a single shirt.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبَانُ بْنُ صَمْعَةَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فِي الْقَمِيصِ الْوَاحِدِ، إِذَا كَانَ صَفِيقًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abān ibn Ṣam‘ah → ‘Ikrimah → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Abān ibn Ṣam‘ah narrated to us, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: “There is no harm in praying in a single shirt if it is thick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ السَّرَّاجِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ صَلَّى فِي قَمِيصٍ، لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ غَيْرُهُ

english.narrator: Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Ismā‘īl al-Sarrāj → Mujāhid → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Ismā‘īl al-Sarrāj, from Mujāhid, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he prayed in a shirt with nothing else on him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ يَزِيدَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا أُمَامَةَ، وَسُئِلَ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي الْقَمِيصِ الْوَاحِدِ، فَقَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ، وَفِي الرَّيْطَةِ إِذَا تَوَشَّحْتُ بِهَا، فَلَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb → Mu‘āwiyah ibn Ṣāliḥ → Mūsā ibn Yazīd → Abū Umāmah
english.text: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb narrated to us, from Mu‘āwiyah ibn Ṣāliḥ, from Mūsā ibn Yazīd, who said: I heard Abū Umāmah being asked about praying in a single shirt. He said: “There is no harm in it. And in a Raiṭah (a single piece of cloth), if you wrap it around yourself, there is no harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى، قَالَ: سُئِلَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي قَمِيصٍ وَاحِدٍ، فَقَالَ: رُبَّ رَجُلٍ لَيْسَ لَهُ إِلَّا قَمِيصٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Manṣūr → Abū al-Ḍuḥā → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Abū al-Ḍuḥā, who said: Ibn ‘Abbās was asked about praying in a single shirt. He said: “Many a man has only a shirt.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ فِي الْقَمِيصِ الْوَاحِدِ، إِذَا كَانَ صَفِيقًا

english.narrator: Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Al-A‘mash → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Abū Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “There is no harm in a single shirt if it is thick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: أَمَّنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ فِي قَمِيصٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Shu‘bah → Sa‘d ibn Ibrāhīm → His Father → Mu‘āwiyah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Shu‘bah narrated to us, from Sa‘d ibn Ibrāhīm, from his father, who said: “Mu‘āwiyah led us in prayer in a shirt.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ أَنَّهُ صَلَّى فِي قَمِيصٍ

english.narrator: Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Al-A‘mash → Sa‘d ibn ‘Ubaydah → Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān
english.text: Abū Mu‘āwiyah narrated to us, from Al-A‘mash, from Sa‘d ibn ‘Ubaydah, from Abū ‘Abd al-Raḥmān, that he prayed in a shirt.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْلَى بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، قَالَ: بَعَثْتُ غُلَامًا لِي كَاتِبًا حَاسِبًا إِلَى سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، يَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي قَمِيصٍ لَيْسَ تَحْتَهُ إِزَارٌ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ إِذَا لَمْ يَكْشِفْ عَنْهُ

english.narrator: Ya‘lā ibn ‘Ubayd → Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq → Sa‘īd ibn Abī Hind → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Ya‘lā ibn ‘Ubayd narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq, from Sa‘īd ibn Abī Hind, who said: I sent a servant of mine, a writer and accountant, to Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib, asking him about praying in a shirt with no lower garment underneath. He said: “There is no harm in it if it does not uncover him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ الْجُرَيْرِيِّ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا بِالصَّلَاةِ فِي الْقَمِيصِ الْوَاحِدِ خَصِيفًا

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Al-Jurayrī → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Al-Jurayrī, from ‘Ikrimah, that he saw no harm in praying in a single thick shirt.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنِ الْهُذَيْلِ بْنِ بِلَالٍ الْفَزَارِيِّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي زِيَادُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ الْأَحْمَرِيُّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عَلْقَمَةَ، يُصَلِّي فِي قَمِيصٍ ضَيِّقٍ قَصِيرٍ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn al-Ḥubāb → Al-Hudhayl ibn Bilāl al-Fazārī → Ziyād ibn ‘Uthmān al-Aḥmarī → ‘Alqamah
english.text: Zayd ibn al-Ḥubāb narrated to us, from Al-Hudhayl ibn Bilāl al-Fazārī, who said: Ziyād ibn ‘Uthmān al-Aḥmarī narrated to me, saying: “I saw ‘Alqamah praying in a tight, short shirt.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: نا الْعَوَّامُ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي رَبَاحٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فِي ثَوْبٍ وَاحِدٍ، إِذَا كَانَ صَفِيقًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Al-‘Awwām → ‘Aṭā’ ibn Abī Rabāḥ
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-‘Awwām narrated to us, from ‘Aṭā’ ibn Abī Rabāḥ, who said: “There is no harm in praying in a single garment if it is thick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: أنا أَبُو إِسْرَائِيلَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنْ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فِي الثَّوْبِ الْوَاحِدِ، إِذَا كَانَ صَفِيقًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abū Isrā’īl Ismā‘īl → Fuḍayl ibn ‘Amr
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Abū Isrā’īl Ismā‘īl informed us, from Fuḍayl ibn ‘Amr, who said: “There is no harm in praying in a single garment if it is thick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، قَالَ: أَمَّنَا الْحَكَمُ فِي قَمِيصٍ غَلِيظٍ، وَقَالَ الْحَكَمُ: لَا بَأْسَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فِي الْقَمِيصِ الْوَاحِدِ، إِذَا كَانَ صَفِيقًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, who said: Al-Ḥakam led us in prayer in a thick shirt. Al-Ḥakam said: “There is no harm in praying in a single shirt if it is thick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ لَهِيعَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ نَوْفَلٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُرْوَةَ بْنَ الزُّبَيْرِ، يُصَلِّي فِي قَمِيصٍ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ غَيْرُهُ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb → Ibn Lahī‘ah → Muḥammad ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nawfal → ‘Urwah ibn al-Zubayr
english.text: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb narrated to us, from Ibn Lahī‘ah, from Muḥammad ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Nawfal, who said: “I saw ‘Urwah ibn al-Zubayr praying in a shirt with nothing else on him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ هَرِمٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ جَابِرُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي فِي جُبَّةٍ وَحْدَهَا، أَوْ قَمِيصٍ صَفِيقٍ يُوَارِي عَوْرَتَهُ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ غَيْرُهُ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Ḥabīb → ‘Amr ibn Harim → Jābir ibn Zayd
english.text: Yazīd ibn Hārūn narrated to us, from Ḥabīb, from ‘Amr ibn Harim, who said: Jābir ibn Zayd was asked about a man praying in a Jubbah alone, or a thick shirt that covers his ‘Awrah with nothing else on him. He said: “There is no harm in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ أَبِي حُصَيْنٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَتْنِي مُلَيْكَةُ بِنْتُ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، أَنَّ أَبَاهَا كَانَ يُصَلِّي فِي قَمِيصٍ تَطَوَّعَا بِاللَّيْلِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh → Abū Ḥuṣayn → Mulaykah bint Abī ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → Her Father
english.text: Abū Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh narrated to us, from Abū Ḥuṣayn, who said: Mulaykah bint Abī ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to me that her father used to pray voluntary prayers at night in a shirt.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، أَنَّ سَعْدًا صَلَّى بِالنَّاسِ فِي مُسْتُقَةٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ → Layth → Al-Ḥakam → Sa‘d
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Layth, from Al-Ḥakam, that Sa‘d led the people in prayer wearing a Mustaqah (a type of fur coat).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبَانُ بْنُ صَمْعَةَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فِي الْجُبَّةِ الْوَاحِدَةِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abān ibn Ṣam‘ah → ‘Ikrimah → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Abān ibn Ṣam‘ah narrated to us, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: “There is no harm in praying in a single Jubbah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَائِدَةُ بْنُ قُدَامَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ جُدْعَانَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ الْمُسَيِّبِ عَنْ الصَّلَاةِ فِي الْجُبَّةِ، قَالَ: وَفِي الْقَمِيصِ إِذَا كَانَ صَفِيقًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Zā’idah ibn Qudāmah → ‘Alī ibn Zayd ibn Jud‘ān → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Zā’idah ibn Qudāmah narrated to us, from ‘Alī ibn Zayd ibn Jud‘ān, who said: I asked Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib about praying in a Jubbah. He said: “And in a shirt if it is thick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا الرَّبِيعُ بْنُ صَبِيحٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي مَنْ رَأَى عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ يُصَلِّي فِي جُبَّةٍ طَيَالِسَةٍ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ إِزَارٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Al-Rabī‘ ibn Ṣabīḥ → Someone → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-Rabī‘ ibn Ṣabīḥ informed us, saying: Someone who saw ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz praying in a Ṭayālisah Jubbah with no Izār on him informed me.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُحِلٍّ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ يُصَلِّي فِي مُسْتُقَةٍ لَا يُخْرِجُ يَدَيْهِ مِنْهَا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Muḥill → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Muḥill, who said: “I saw Ibrāhīm praying in a Mustaqah without taking his hands out of it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ خُصَيْفٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: أَيُّمَا امْرَأَةٍ صَلَّتْ وَلَمْ تُغَطِّ شَعْرَهَا، لَمْ تُقْبَلْ لَهَا صَلَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl → Khuṣayf → Mujāhid
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Muḥammad ibn Fuḍayl narrated to us, from Khuṣayf, from Mujāhid, who said: “Any woman who prays without covering her hair, her prayer will not be accepted.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، رَفَعَهُ قَالَ: إِذَا حَاضَتْ الْجَارِيَةُ لَمْ تُقْبَلْ لَهَا صَلَاةٌ إِلَّا بِخِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus → ‘Amr → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Īsā ibn Yūnus narrated to us, from ‘Amr, from Al-Ḥasan, raising it (to the Prophet), who said: “If a girl reaches menstruation, no prayer will be accepted from her except with a veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ قَابُوسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ أَرْسَلَ امْرَأَةً إِلَى عَائِشَةَ، فَرَأَتْ جَارِيَةً لَهَا جَمَّةٌ، فَقَالَتْ: لَوِ اسْتَتَرَتْ هَذِهِ كَانَ أَحْرَى بِهَا فَقَالَتْ: إِنَّهَا لَمْ تَحِضْ، وَلَا بَدَا بَعْدُ الْحَيْضُ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Qābūs → His Father → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Qābūs, from his father, that he sent a woman to ‘Ā’ishah. She saw a girl with long hair and said: “If this one were to cover herself, it would be more appropriate for her.” She said: “She has not menstruated, nor has menstruation appeared yet.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ، أَنَّ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: دَخَلَ عَلَيَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَعِنْدِي فَتَاةٌ، فَأَلْقَى إِلَيَّ حِقْوَهُ، فَقَالَ: شُقِّيهِ بَيْنَ هَذِهِ الْفَتَاةِ وَبَيْنَ الَّتِي عِنْدَ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، فَإِنِّي لَا أَرَاهُمَا إِلَّا قَدْ حَاضَتَا

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām → Muḥammad → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām, from Muḥammad, that ‘Ā’ishah said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered upon me while a young girl was with me. He threw his waist wrapper to me and said: “Tear it between this girl and the one with Umm Salamah, for I do not see them except that they have reached menstruation.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا، فَاخْتَبَأَتْ مَوْلَاةٌ لَهُمْ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: حَاضَتْ؟ فَقَالُوا: نَعَمْ، فَشَقَّ لَهَا مِنْ عِمَامَتِهِ، فَقَالَ: اخْتَمِرِي بِهَذَا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → ‘Abd al-Karīm → ‘Amr ibn Sa‘īd → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-Karīm, from ‘Amr ibn Sa‘īd, from ‘Ā’ishah, that the Prophet ﷺ entered upon her, and a freed slave girl of theirs hid. The Prophet ﷺ said: “Has she menstruated?” They said: “Yes.” He tore a piece from his turban for her and said: “Veil yourself with this.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ الثَّقَفِيِّ، عَنْ مَاهَانَ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَتْ: إِذَا حَاضَتِ الْجَارِيَةُ وَجَبَ عَلَيْهَا مَا وَجَبَ عَلَى أُمِّهَا مِنَ التَّسَتُّرِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sharīk → ‘Uthmān al-Thaqafī → Māhān → Umm Salamah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sharīk narrated to us, from ‘Uthmān al-Thaqafī, from Māhān, from Umm Salamah, who said: “When a girl menstruates, what is obligatory upon her mother regarding covering becomes obligatory upon her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْزُوقُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، مَوْلَى بَنِي زُهْرَةَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ الْمُسَيِّبِ، مَتَى تُكْتَبُ عَلَى الْجَارِيَةِ الصَّلَاةُ؟ فَقَالَ: إِذَا حَاضَتْ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Marzūq ibn ‘Abd Allāh (Mawla Bani Zuhrah) → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Marzūq ibn ‘Abd Allāh, the freed slave of Banī Zuhrah, narrated to us, saying: I asked Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib: “When is prayer prescribed for a girl?” He said: “When she menstruates.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: إِذَا حَاضَتِ الْجَارِيَةُ وَجَبَ عَلَيْهَا مَا وَجَبَ عَلَى أُمِّهَا مِنَ التَّسَتُّرِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyān narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “When a girl menstruates, what is obligatory upon her mother regarding covering becomes obligatory upon her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا رَبِيعٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا حَاضَتِ الْجَارِيَةُ لَمْ تُقْبَلْ لَهَا صَلَاةٌ إِلَّا بِخِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Rabī‘ → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, saying: Rabī‘ narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “If a girl menstruates, no prayer will be accepted from her except with a veil.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ خُصَيْفٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: إِذَا حَاضَتِ الْجَارِيَةُ لَمْ تُقْبَلْ لَهَا صَلَاةٌ إِلَّا بِخِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Khusayf → Mujahid
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Khusayf, from Mujahid, who said: “When a girl menstruates, no prayer is accepted from her except with a headcover (Khimar).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنْ مَاهَانَ أَبِي سَالِمٍ، قَالَ: قَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ: إِذَا احْتَلَمَتْ الْجَارِيَةُ وَجَبَ عَلَيْهَا مَا وَجَبَ عَلَى أُمِّهَا يَعْنِي مِنَ التَّسَتُّرِ

english.narrator: Ibn Idris → Al-Shaybani → Mahan Abu Salim → ‘Aishah
english.text: Ibn Idris narrated to us from Al-Shaybani, from Mahan Abu Salim, who said: ‘Aishah said: “When a girl reaches puberty (has a wet dream), what is obligatory upon her mother becomes obligatory upon her,” meaning covering up.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ صَفِيَّةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: لَا يَقْبَلُ اللَّهُ صَلَاةَ حَائِضٍ إِلَّا بِخِمَارٍ

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Adam → Hammad ibn Salamah → Qatadah → Ibn Sirin → Safiyyah → ‘Aishah
english.text: Yahya ibn Adam narrated to us from Hammad ibn Salamah, from Qatadah, from Ibn Sirin, from Safiyyah, from ‘Aishah, that the Prophet ﷺ said: “Allah does not accept the prayer of a menstruating woman (one who has reached puberty) except with a headcover.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا بَلَغَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ الْحَيْضَ وَلَمْ تُغَطِّ أُذُنَهَا وَرَأْسَهَا، لَمْ تُقْبَلْ لَهَا صَلَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu‘tamir → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Mu‘tamir narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, who said: “If a woman reaches menstruation age and does not cover her ears and head, no prayer is accepted from her.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْأَمَةُ كَمَا تَخْرُجُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah → Abu Usamah → Mujalid → Al-Sha‘bi → Masruq
english.text: Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Mujalid, from Al-Sha‘bi, from Masruq, who said: “The slave woman prays as she goes out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا، وَشُرَيْحًا كَانَا يَقُولَانِ: تُصَلِّي الْأَمَةُ كَمَا تَخْرُجُ

english.narrator: Sharik → Abu Ishaq → ‘Ali/Shurayh
english.text: Sharik narrated to us from Abu Ishaq, that ‘Ali and Shurayh used to say: “The slave woman prays as she goes out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي أُمُّ الْوَلَدِ بِغَيْرِ خِمَارٍ، وَإِنْ كَانَتْ قَدْ بَلَغَتْ سِتِّينَ سَنَةً

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: “The Umm Walad (slave mother) prays without a headcover, even if she has reached sixty years of age.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْأَمَةِ خِمَارٌ وَإِنْ كَانَتْ عَجُوزًا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Hammad → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Hammad, from Ibrahim, who said: “There is no headcover upon the slave woman, even if she is an old woman.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْأَمَةِ خِمَارٌ وَإِنْ كَانَتْ عَجُوزًا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Layth → Mujahid
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Layth, from Mujahid, who said: “There is no headcover upon the slave woman, even if she is an old woman.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْأَمَةِ خِمَارٌ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Layth → Mujahid
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Layth, from Mujahid, who said: “There is no headcover upon the slave woman.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْأَمَةُ كَمَا تَخْرُجُ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Mujalid → Al-Sha‘bi → Masruq
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Mujalid, from Al-Sha‘bi, from Masruq, who said: “The slave woman prays as she goes out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْأَمَةُ كَمَا تَخْرُجُ

english.narrator: Jarir → Mughirah → Al-Harith
english.text: Jarir narrated to us from Mughirah, from Al-Harith, who said: “The slave woman prays as she goes out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: تُصَلِّي الْأَمَةُ كَمَا تَخْرُجُ

english.narrator: ‘Abdah ibn Sulayman → Mujalid → Al-Sha‘bi → Shurayh
english.text: ‘Abdah ibn Sulayman narrated to us from Mujalid, from Al-Sha‘bi, from Shurayh, who said: “The slave woman prays as she goes out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْأَمَةِ خِمَارٌ، وَإِنْ وَلَدَتْ مِنْ سَيِّدِهَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Isra’il → Jabir → ‘Amir
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Isra’il narrated to us from Jabir, from ‘Amir, who said: “There is no headcover upon the slave woman, even if she has given birth to a child by her master.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: أنا إِسْرَائِيلُ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِنَّ الْأَمَةَ قَدْ أَلْقَتْ فَرْوَةَ رَأْسِهَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Isra’il → Jabir → ‘Ata’
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Isra’il informed us from Jabir, from ‘Ata’, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Indeed, the slave woman has cast off the covering of her head.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: رَأَى عُمَرُ أَمَةً لَنَا مُتَقَنِّعَةً، فَضَرَبَهَا وَقَالَ: لَا تَشَبَّهِي بِالْحَرَائِرِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Shu‘bah → Qatadah → Anas
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Shu‘bah narrated to us from Qatadah, from Anas, who said: ‘Umar saw a slave woman of ours wearing a veil, so he struck her and said: “Do not imitate free women.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: إِنَّ الْأَمَةَ قَدْ أَلْقَتْ فَرْوَةَ رَأْسِهَا مِنْ وَرَاءِ الْجِدَارِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Shu‘bah → Al-Hakam → Mujahid
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Shu‘bah narrated to us from Al-Hakam, from Mujahid, who said: ‘Umar said: “Indeed, the slave woman has cast off the covering of her head from behind the wall.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ الْمَخْزُومِيِّ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، بِمِثْلِ حَدِيثِ وَكِيعٍ عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Hajjaj → ‘Ikrimah ibn Khalid al-Makhzumi → ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Hajjaj, from ‘Ikrimah ibn Khalid al-Makhzumi, from ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab, with the like of the hadith of Waki‘ from Shu‘bah from Al-Hakam.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرِ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: رَأَى عُمَرُ جَارِيَةً مُتَقَنِّعَةً، فَضَرَبَهَا وَقَالَ: لَا تَشَبَّهِينَ بِالْحَرَائِرِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘la → Ma‘mar → Al-Zuhri → Anas
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘la narrated to us from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhri, from Anas, who said: ‘Umar saw a girl wearing a veil, so he struck her and said: “Do not imitate free women.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ الْمُخْتَارِ بْنِ فُلْفُلٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: دَخَلَتْ عَلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ أَمَةٌ، قَدْ كَانَ يُعَرِّفُهَا لِبَعْضِ الْمُهَاجِرِينَ، أَوْ الْأَنْصَارِ، وَعَلَيْهَا جِلْبَابٌ مُتَقَنِّعَةً بِهِ، فَسَأَلَهَا عَتَقَتْ؟ قَالَتْ: لَا، قَالَ: فَمَا بَالُ الْجِلْبَابِ، ضَعِيهِ عَنْ رَأْسِكَ، إِنَّمَا الْجِلْبَابُ عَلَى الْحَرَائِرِ مِنْ نِسَاءِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَتَلَكَّأَتْ، فَقَامَ إِلَيْهَا بِالدُّرَّةِ فَضَرَبَ بِهَا بِرَأْسِهَا، حَتَّى أَلْقَتْهُ عَنْ رَأْسِهَا

english.narrator: ‘Ali ibn Mushir → Al-Mukhtar ibn Fulful → Anas ibn Malik
english.text: ‘Ali ibn Mushir narrated to us from Al-Mukhtar ibn Fulful, from Anas ibn Malik, who said: A slave woman entered upon ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab, whom he recognized as belonging to some of the Muhajirun or Ansar. She was wearing a Jilbab and veiling herself with it. He asked her: “Have you been freed?” She said: “No.” He said: “What is with the Jilbab? Remove it from your head. The Jilbab is only for the free women among the believers.” She hesitated, so he went up to her with the whip and struck her head with it until she cast it from her head.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلَهُ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ كَيْفَ تُصَلِّي الْأَمَةُ؟ قَالَ: تُصَلِّي كَمَا تَخْرُجُ

english.narrator: Hafs → Mujalid → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Hafs narrated to us from Mujalid, from Al-Sha‘bi, who said: Abu Hurayrah asked him how the slave woman prays. He said: “She prays as she goes out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، قَالَ: كَانَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ لَا يَدْعُ فِي خِلَافَتِهِ أَمَةً تَقَنَّعُ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: إِنَّمَا الْقِنَاعُ لِلْحَرَائِرِ لَكَيْلَا لَا يُؤْذَيْنَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Khalid → Abu Qilabah
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Khalid, from Abu Qilabah, who said: During his caliphate, ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab would not let a slave woman veil herself. He said: ‘Umar said: “The veil is only for free women so that they may not be harmed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أنا عَوْفٌ، قَالَ: "قَدِمَ عَامِلٌ لِمُعَاوِيَةَ، وَكَانَ بَعَثَهُ عَلَى الصَّدَقَاتِ، فَنَزَلَ مَنْزِلًا، فَإِذَا هُوَ بِمَسْجِدَيْنِ، قَالَ: أَيُّهُمَا أَقْدَمُ، فَأُخْبِرَ بِهِ، فَأَتَى الَّذِي هُوَ أَقْدَمُهُمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → ‘Awf
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: ‘Awf informed us, saying: A worker of Mu‘awiyah arrived—he had sent him to collect alms. He stopped at a place and found two mosques. He said: “Which of them is older?” He was told about it, so he went to the one which was older of the two.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، أَنَّ أَبَا وَائِلٍ، فَاتَتْهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِي مَسْجِدِ كَذَا وَكَذَا، فَصَلَّى فِي مَسْجِدِ كَذَا وَكَذَا، وَبَيْنَهُمَا مَسَاجِدُ كَثِيرَةٌ مُحْدَثَةٌ لَمْ يُصَلِّ فِيهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abd al-Salam ibn Harb → Layth
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Salam ibn Harb narrated to us from Layth, that Abu Wa’il missed the prayer in such-and-such mosque, so he prayed in such-and-such mosque, and between them were many newly built mosques in which he did not pray.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عُمَارَةَ الصَّيْدَلَانِيِّ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ الْبُنَانِيِّ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَكُونُ مَعَ أَنَسٍ، فَيَأْتِي عَلَى الْمَسْجِدِ فَيَسْمَعُ الْأَذَانَ، فَيَقُولُ: مُحْدَثٌ هَذَا؟ فَإِذَا قَالُوا: نَعَمْ، يُجَاوِزُهُ إِلَى غَيْرِهِ

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman → ‘Umarah al-Saydalani → Thabit al-Bunani
english.text: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman narrated to us from ‘Umarah al-Saydalani, from Thabit al-Bunani, who said: I used to be with Anas. He would come upon a mosque and hear the Adhan, and say: “Is this newly built?” If they said: “Yes,” he would pass it by to another.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَتَجَاوَزُ الْمَسَاجِدَ الْمُحْدَثَةَ إِلَى الْقَدِيمَةِ

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir → Layth → Mujahid
english.text: Mu‘tamir narrated to us from Layth, from Mujahid, that he used to pass by the newly built mosques to go to the old ones.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرٌ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي رَجُلٌ، مِنْ أَهْلِ الْبَادِيَةِ، قَالَ: قَدِمَ عَلَيْنَا مُصَدِّقٌ مِنَ الْمَدِينَةِ لَيَالِي مُعَاوِيَةَ، فَبَيْنَمَا هُوَ عَلَى مَاءٍ لَنَا ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ قَالَ: وَحَضَرَتِ الصَّلَاةُ، وَعَلَى الْمَاءِ مَسْجِدَانِ مِنْ مَسَاجِدِ أَهْلِ الْبَادِيَةِ، قَالَ: أَيُّهُمَا بُنِيَ أَوَّلًا؟ فَقِيلَ: هَذَا، فَقَصَدَ نَحْوَهُ

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir → ‘Awf → A man from the desert dwellers
english.text: Mu‘tamir narrated to us from ‘Awf, who said: A man from the desert dwellers informed me, saying: A collector of Zakat came to us from Madinah during the nights of Mu‘awiyah. While he was at a water source of ours one day, the time for prayer came. At the water source, there were two mosques of the desert dwellers. He said: “Which of them was built first?” It was said: “This one.” So he headed towards it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: نا مَنْصُورٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَدَعُ مَسْجِدَ قَوْمِهِ وَيَأْتِي غَيْرَهُ، قَالَ: فَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ: كَانُوا يُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يُكَثِّرَ الرَّجُلُ قَوْمَهُ بِنَفْسِهِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mansur → Al-Hasan
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: Mansur narrated to us from Al-Hasan, that he was asked about a man who leaves the mosque of his people and goes to another. Al-Hasan said: “They used to like for a man to increase the number of his people by his presence.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ قَابُوسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ فَرَكَعَ فِيهِ رَكْعَةً، فَقَالُوا لَهُ: فَقَالَ: إِنَّمَا هُوَ تَطَوُّعٌ، فَمِنْ شَاءَ نَقَصَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Qabus → His Father
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Jarir narrated to us from Qabus, from his father, that ‘Umar entered the mosque and bowed one rak‘ah in it. They spoke to him, so he said: “It is only voluntary, so whoever wishes may decrease.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ قَابُوسٍ، عَنْ أَبِي ظَبْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنْ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ مَرَّ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فَرَكَعَ رَكْعَةً، فَقِيلَ لَهُ: إِنَّمَا رَكَعْتَ رَكْعَةً، فَقَالَ: إِنَّمَا هُوَ تَطَوُّعٌ، وَكَرِهْتُ أَنْ أَتَّخِذَهُ طَرِيقًا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Qabus → Abu Zabyan → His Father
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Qabus, from Abu Zabyan, from his father, that ‘Umar ibn al-Khattab passed through the mosque and bowed one rak‘ah. It was said to him: “You only bowed one rak‘ah.” He said: “It is only voluntary, and I disliked using it as a pathway.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي مِنْ رَأَى طَلْحَةَ بْنَ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ مَرَّ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فَرَكَعَ رَكْعَةً، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ

english.narrator: Sharik → Simak → Someone who saw Talhah ibn ‘Ubayd Allah
english.text: Sharik narrated to us from Simak, who said: Someone narrated to me who saw Talhah ibn ‘Ubayd Allah pass through the mosque, bow one rak‘ah, then leave.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الزُّبَيْرَ بْنَ الْعَوَّامِ خَرَجَ مِنَ الْقَصْرِ فَمَرَّ بِالْمَسْجِدِ، فَرَكَعَ رَكْعَةً، أَوْ سَجَدَ سَجْدَةً

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sayf ibn Maysarah → Abu Sa‘id
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sayf ibn Maysarah, from Abu Sa‘id, who said: “I saw Al-Zubayr ibn al-‘Awwam come out of the palace, pass by the mosque, and bow one rak‘ah, or prostrate one prostration.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ الْأَحْوَصِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ، عَنْ رَاشِدِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، يُصَلُّونَ وَعَلَيْهِمْ قِسِيُّهُمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Usamah → Al-Ahwas ibn Hakim → Rashid ibn Sa‘d
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Al-Ahwas ibn Hakim, from Rashid ibn Sa‘d, who said: “The Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pray while carrying their bows.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ الْأَحْوَصِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي رَاشِدُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، قَالَ: "كَانَ يُقَالُ: السُّيُوفُ أَرِدْيَةُ الْغُزَاةِ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Al-Ahwas ibn Hakim → Rashid ibn Sa‘d → ‘Urwah ibn al-Zubayr
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Al-Ahwas ibn Hakim, who said: Rashid ibn Sa‘d narrated to me from ‘Urwah ibn al-Zubayr, who said: “It used to be said: Swords are the cloaks of the warriors.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانُوا يَرَوْنَ أَنَّ السُّيُوفَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الرِّدَاءِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Sufyan narrated to us from Mansur, from Ibrahim, who said: “They used to consider swords as being like a cloak in prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْأَسَدِيُّ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ مَسْرُوقٍ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ التَّيْمِيَّ يُصَلِّي وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ رِدَاءٌ إِلَّا سَيْفُهُ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Asadi → Isra’il → Sa‘id ibn Masruq
english.text: Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Asadi narrated to us from Isra’il, from Sa‘id ibn Masruq, who said: “I saw Ibrahim al-Taymi praying with no cloak upon him except his sword.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدَةُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَصْحَابُ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلُّونَ فِي السُّيُوفِ عَلَيْهَا الْكَمْخَتُ مِنْ جُلُودِ الْمَيْتَةِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → ‘Ubaydah → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: ‘Ubaydah narrated to us from Ibrahim, who said: “The Companions of Muhammad ﷺ used to pray while wearing swords that had Kamkhat (sheaths) made from the skins of dead animals.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الرَّبِيعُ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: السُّيُوفُ أَرِدْيَةُ الْغُزَاةِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Al-Rabi‘ → Al-Hasan
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-Rabi‘ narrated to us from Al-Hasan, who said: “Swords are the cloaks of the warriors.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: الْقَوْسُ لَا يُجْزِي مَكَانَ الرِّدَاءِ

english.narrator: Jarir → Mis‘ar → Hammad
english.text: Jarir narrated to us from Mis‘ar, from Hammad, who said: “The bow does not suffice in place of the cloak.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: أنا عُبَيْدَةُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: الْقَوْسُ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الرِّدَاءِ

english.narrator: Hushaym → ‘Ubaydah → Ibrahim
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: ‘Ubaydah informed us from Ibrahim, who said: “The bow is in the position of the cloak.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عُقْبَةُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبِي، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ الْأَكْوَعِ، أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ فِي الْقَوْسِ وَالْقَرْنِ؟ فَقَالَ: صَلِّ فِي الْقَوْسِ، وَاطْرَحْ الْقَرْنَ

english.narrator: ‘Uqbah ibn Khalid → Musa ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim → His Father → Salamah ibn al-Akwa‘
english.text: ‘Uqbah ibn Khalid narrated to us from Musa ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim, who said: My father informed me from Salamah ibn al-Akwa‘, that he asked the Prophet ﷺ about praying with a bow and a horn (quiver). He said: “Pray with the bow, and cast aside the horn.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: "إِذَا كَانَ لَيْلَةٌ مَطِيرَةٌ أَوْ شَدِيدَةُ الرِّيحِ أَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مُنَادِيًا، فَيُنَادِي: أَنْ صَلُّوا فِي رِحَالِكُمْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Ibn Abi Layla → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hushaym narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: “When it was a rainy or very windy night, the Prophet ﷺ would order a caller to call out: ‘Pray in your dwellings.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمَلِيحِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ عَامَ الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ أَوْ حُنَيْنٍ، فَأَصَابَنَا مَطَرٌ لَمْ يَبُلَّ أَسَافِلَ نِعَالِنَا فَنَادَى، مُنَادِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَنْ صَلُّوا فِي رِحَالِكُمْ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Khalid → Abu al-Malih → His Father
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us from Khalid, from Abu al-Malih, from his father, who said: “I was with the Prophet ﷺ in the year of Hudaybiyyah or Hunayn. Rain fell on us which did not soak the soles of our sandals, so the caller of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ called out: ‘Pray in your dwellings.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَوْفٌ، عَنْ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ، قَالَ: "أَصَابَنَا مَطَرٌ فِي يَوْمِ جُمُعَةٍ، فِي عَهْدِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فَأَمَرَ مُنَادِيًا، فَنَادَى: أَنْ صَلُّوا فِي رِحَالِكُمْ

english.narrator: Hushaym → ‘Awf → Abu Raja’
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, saying: ‘Awf narrated to us from Abu Raja’, who said: “Rain fell on us on a Friday during the time of Ibn ‘Abbas, so he ordered a caller who called out: ‘Pray in your dwellings.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمَلِيحِ، قَالَ: خَرَجْتُ ذَاتَ لَيْلَةٍ مَطِيرَةٍ إِلَى الْمَسْجِدِ، فَلَمَّا رَكَعْتُ اسْتَفْتَحْتُ، قَالَ أَبِي: مَنْ هَذَا؟ قَالُوا: أَبُو الْمَلِيحِ، قَالَ: "لَقَدْ رَأَيْتُنَا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَوْمَ الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ، وَأَصَابَتْنَا سَمَاءٌ لَمْ تَبُلَّ أَسَافِلَ نِعَالِنَا فَنَادَى مُنَادِي رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَنْ صَلُّوا فِي رِحَالِكُمْ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Khalid → Abu Qilabah → Abu al-Malih
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Khalid, from Abu Qilabah, from Abu al-Malih, who said: I went out one rainy night to the mosque. When I bowed and began the prayer, my father said: “Who is this?” They said: “Abu al-Malih.” He said: “I saw us with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on the day of Hudaybiyyah, and rain fell on us which did not soak the soles of our sandals, so the caller of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ called out: ‘Pray in your dwellings.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هَمَّامٌ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ سَمُرَةَ بْنِ جُنْدُبٍ، "أَنَّ يَوْمَ حُنَيْنٍ كَانَ يَوْمًا مَطِيرًا، فَأَمَرَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مُنَادِيَهُ: أَنَّ الصَّلَاةَ فِي الرِّحَالِ

english.narrator: ‘Affan → Hammam → Qatadah → Al-Hasan → Samurah ibn Jundub
english.text: ‘Affan narrated to us, saying: Hammam narrated to us from Qatadah, from Al-Hasan, from Samurah ibn Jundub, that the day of Hunayn was a rainy day, so the Prophet ﷺ ordered his caller [to announce] that prayer is in the dwellings.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: كَانَتْ أُمَرَاؤُنَا إِذَا كَانَتْ لَيْلَةٌ مَطِيرَةٌ أَبْطَأُوا بِالْمَغْرِبِ، وَعَجَّلُوا بِالْعِشَاءِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغِيبَ الشَّفَقُ، فَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يُصَلِّي مَعَهُمْ، لَا يَرَى بِذَلِكَ بَأْسًا قَالَ عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ: وَرَأَيْتُ الْقَاسِمَ، وَسَالِمًا يُصَلِّيَانِ مَعَهُمْ فِي مِثْلِ تِلْكَ اللَّيْلَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Usamah → ‘Ubayd Allah → Nafi‘
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us, saying: ‘Ubayd Allah narrated to us from Nafi‘, who said: “Our rulers, if it was a rainy night, would delay Maghrib and hasten ‘Isha before the twilight disappeared. Ibn ‘Umar used to pray with them and saw no harm in that.” ‘Ubayd Allah said: “And I saw Al-Qasim and Salim praying with them on such a night.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ حَرْمَلَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ الْمُسَيِّبِ يُصَلِّي مَعَ الْأَئِمَّةِ حِينَ يَجْمَعُونَ بَيْنَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَالْعِشَاءِ فِي اللَّيْلَةِ الْمَطِيرَةِ

english.narrator: Hatim ibn Isma‘il → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Harmalah
english.text: Hatim ibn Isma‘il narrated to us from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Harmalah, who said: “I saw Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib praying with the Imams when they combined Maghrib and ‘Isha on a rainy night.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ بِلَالٍ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَانَ بْنَ عُثْمَانَ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَ الصَّلَاتَيْنِ فِي اللَّيْلَةِ الْمَطِيرَةِ، الْمَغْرِبِ وَالْعِشَاءِ فَيُصَلِّيهِمَا مَعًا عُرْوَةُ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيِّبِ، وَأَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، وَأَبُو سَلَمَةَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، لَا يُنْكِرُونَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Sulayman ibn Bilal → Hisham ibn ‘Urwah
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Sulayman ibn Bilal, from Hisham ibn ‘Urwah, who said: “I saw Aban ibn ‘Uthman combine the two prayers on a rainy night, Maghrib and ‘Isha, praying them together.” ‘Urwah ibn al-Zubayr, Sa‘id ibn al-Musayyib, Abu Bakr ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman, and Abu Salamah ibn ‘Abd al-Rahman did not disapprove of it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ خَالِدِ، عَنْ أَبِي مَوْدُودٍ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ، فَجَمَعَ بَيْنَهُمَا فِي اللَّيْلَةِ الْمَطِيرَةِ

english.narrator: Hammad ibn Khalid → Abu Mawdud ‘Abd al-‘Aziz ibn Abi Sulayman
english.text: Hammad ibn Khalid narrated to us from Abu Mawdud ‘Abd al-‘Aziz ibn Abi Sulayman, who said: “I prayed Maghrib and ‘Isha with Abu Bakr ibn Muhammad, and he combined them on a rainy night.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يُصَلِّي مَعَ مَرْوَانَ، وَكَانَ مَرْوَانُ إِذَا كَانَتْ لَيْلَةٌ مَطِيرَةٌ جَمَعَ بَيْنَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَالْعِشَاءِ، وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يُصَلِّيهِمَا مَعَهُ

english.narrator: Muhammad ibn ‘Ubayd → Muhammad ibn Ishaq → Nafi‘
english.text: Muhammad ibn ‘Ubayd narrated to us from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from Nafi‘, who said: “Ibn ‘Umar used to pray with Marwan. When it was a rainy night, Marwan would combine Maghrib and ‘Isha, and Ibn ‘Umar would pray them with him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي مُخْبِرٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، فِي قَوْلِهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى: لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ قَالَ: إِذَا فَاءَ الْفَيْءُ {وَاللَّيْلِ وَمَا وَسَقَ} [الانشقاق: 17] قَالَ: وَمَا جَمَعَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Zayd ibn Hubab → Malik ibn Anas → Dawud → Husayn → A reporter → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Zayd ibn Hubab narrated to us, saying: Malik ibn Anas informed me from Dawud, from Husayn, who said: Someone informed me from Ibn ‘Abbas regarding His saying, Blessed and Exalted: {at the decline of the sun} [Al-Isra: 78], he said: “When the shadow returns.” And {And the night and what it envelops} [Al-Inshiqaq: 17], he said: “And what it gathers.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: "دُلُوكُ الشَّمْسِ: مَيْلُهَا بَعْدَ نِصْفِ النَّهَارِ

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Sa‘id → ‘Ubayd Allah → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us from ‘Ubayd Allah, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: “The decline of the sun (Duluk) is its inclination after midday.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: {أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ إِلَى غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ} [الإسراء: 78] قَالَ: "دُلُوكُهَا: غُرُوبُهَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Mansur → Mujahid → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Mansur, from Mujahid, from Ibn ‘Abbas regarding {Establish prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night} [Al-Isra: 78]. He said: “Its decline (Duluk) is its setting.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي بُكَيْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، {أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ} [الإسراء: 78] قَالَ: "دُلُوكُهَا: قَبْلَ أَنْ تَغِيبَ

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Abi Bukayr → Ibrahim ibn Nafi‘ → Ibn Tawus → His Father
english.text: Yahya ibn Abi Bukayr narrated to us, saying: Ibrahim ibn Nafi‘ narrated to us from Ibn Tawus, from his father regarding {Establish prayer at the decline of the sun} [Al-Isra: 78]. He said: “Its decline (Duluk) is before it sets.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي يُونُسُ بْنُ خَبَّابٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ أَقُودُ مَوْلَايَ السَّائِبَ وَهُوَ أَعْمَى، فَيَقُولُ لِي: يَا مُجَاهِدُ، أَدَلَكَتِ الشَّمْسُ؟ فَإِذَا قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ، قَامَ فَصَلَّى الظُّهْرَ

english.narrator: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Isma‘il ibn ‘Abd al-Malik → Yunus ibn Khabbab → Mujahid
english.text: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, saying: Isma‘il ibn ‘Abd al-Malik narrated to us, saying: Yunus ibn Khabbab narrated to me from Mujahid, who said: I used to lead my master Al-Sa’ib, and he was blind. He would say to me: “O Mujahid, has the sun declined?” If I said: “Yes,” he would stand and pray Zuhr.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ جَالِسًا مَعَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ فِي بَيْتِهِ، فَوَجَبَتِ الشَّمْسُ، فَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: {أَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ إِلَى غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ} [الإسراء: 78] ثُمَّ قَالَ: هَذَا وَاللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا إِلَهَ غَيْرُهُ، حِينَ أَفْطَرَ الصَّائِمُ، وَبَلَغَ وَقْتُ هَذِهِ الصَّلَاةِ

english.narrator: ‘Ali ibn Mushir → Al-Shaybani → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad → His Father
english.text: ‘Ali ibn Mushir narrated to us from Al-Shaybani, from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Aswad, from his father, who said: I was sitting with ‘Abd Allah in his house, and the sun set. ‘Abd Allah recited: {Establish prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night} [Al-Isra: 78]. Then he said: “This, by Allah besides Whom there is no deity, is when the fasting person breaks his fast, and the time for this prayer has arrived.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: "دُلُوكُهَا: مَيْلُهَا

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → ‘Abd al-Hamid ibn Ja‘far → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from ‘Abd al-Hamid ibn Ja‘far, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: “Its decline (Duluk) is its inclination.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ يَعْقُوبَ الْقُمِّيِّ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي الْمُغِيرَةِ، قَالَ: "دُلُوكُهَا: زَوَالُهَا

english.narrator: Ishaq ibn Mansur → Ya‘qub al-Qummi → Ja‘far ibn Abi al-Mughirah
english.text: Ishaq ibn Mansur narrated to us from Ya‘qub al-Qummi, from Ja‘far ibn Abi al-Mughirah, who said: “Its decline is its passing the zenith (Zawal).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ أَبِي كُدَيْنَةَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: "دُلُوكُهَا: زَوَالُهَا

english.narrator: Ishaq ibn Mansur → Abu Kudaynah → Mughirah → Al-Sha‘bi
english.text: Ishaq ibn Mansur narrated to us from Abu Kudaynah, from Mughirah, from Al-Sha‘bi, who said: “Its decline is its passing the zenith.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ، عَنْ وَرْقَاءَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: "دُلُوكُ الشَّمْسِ: تَزِيغُ، وَغَسَقُ اللَّيْلِ: غُرُوبُ الشَّمْسِ

english.narrator: Shababah → Warqa’ → Ibn Abi Najih → Mujahid
english.text: Shababah narrated to us from Warqa’, from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, who said: “The decline of the sun is it passing the zenith, and the darkness of the night is the setting of the sun.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي سِنَانٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: "دُلُوكُهَا: غُرُوبُهَا

english.narrator: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Isma‘il ibn Ishaq → Abu Sinan → Abu Ishaq → ‘Ali
english.text: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, saying: Isma‘il ibn Ishaq narrated to us from Abu Sinan, from Abu Ishaq, from ‘Ali, who said: “Its decline is its setting.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعُمَيْسِ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، "ذَهَبَ بَصَرُ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ فَأُتِيَ بِطَبِيبٍ، فَقَالَ: أُدَاوِيَكَ أَنْ تَسْتَلْقِيَ سَبْعَةَ أَيَّامٍ، وَلَا تُصَلِّي إِلَّا مُضْطَجِعًا، فَأَبَى، وَكَرِهَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Usamah → Abu al-‘Umays → Al-Qasim
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Abu al-‘Umays, from Al-Qasim, that ‘Ubayd Allah ibn ‘Utbah lost his sight. A doctor was brought to him and said: “I will treat you on the condition that you lie on your back for seven days and do not pray except lying down.” He refused and disliked it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ "أَنَّهُ فِي عَيْنَيْهِ الْمَاءُ، فَقِيلَ لَهُ: تَسْتَلْقِي سَبْعًا، فَكَرِهَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Sufyan → ‘Asim → Abu Wa’il
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Sufyan, from ‘Asim, from Abu Wa’il, that there was water in his eyes (cataracts). It was said to him: “Lie on your back for seven [days].” He disliked it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ بْنِ رَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَمَّا كَفَّ بَصَرُهُ أَتَاهُ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ لَهُ: إِنْ دَاوَيْتُكَ لَهُ إِنْ صَبَرْتَ لِي سَبْعًا لَا تُصَلِّي إِلَّا مُسْتَلْقِيًا، دَاوَيْتُكَ وَرَجَوْتُ أَنْ تَبْرَأَ عَيْنُكَ، قَالَ: فَأَرْسَلَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ إِلَى عَائِشَةَ، وَأَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، وَغَيْرِهِمَا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ: كُلُّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ: أَرَأَيْتُ إِنْ مِتُّ فِي هَذِهِ السَّبْعِ كَيْفَ تَصْنَعُ بِالصَّلَاةِ؟ قَالَ: فَتَرَكَ عَيْنَيْهِ لَمْ يَكُ يُدَاوِيهَا

english.narrator: Abu Mu‘awiyah → Al-A‘mash → Al-Musayyib ibn Rafi‘ → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Al-A‘mash, from Al-Musayyib ibn Rafi‘, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who said: When he lost his sight, a man came to him and said: “If I treat you, provided you bear with me for seven days praying only lying down, I will treat you and hope your eye will heal.” Ibn ‘Abbas sent to ‘Aishah, Abu Hurayrah, and others from the Companions of Muhammad ﷺ. He said: All of them said: “What if you die during these seven days? What will you do about the prayer?” So he left his eyes untreated.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الضُّحَى، "أَنَّ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، أُوقِعَ فِي عَيْنَيْهِ الْمَاءُ، فَقِيلَ: أَتَسْتَلْقِي سَبْعًا وَلَا تُصَلِّي إِلَّا مُسْتَلْقِيًا، فَبَعَثَ إِلَى عَائِشَةَ، وَأُمِّ سَلَمَةَ فَسَأَلَهُمَا فَنَهَتَاهُ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdi → Sufyan → Jabir → Abu al-Duha
english.text: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us from Sufyan, from Jabir, from Abu al-Duha, that Ibn ‘Abbas had water fall into his eyes (cataracts). It was said: “Will you lie on your back for seven days and not pray except lying down?” He sent to ‘Aishah and Umm Salamah and asked them, and they forbade him.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْغَيْمِ، فَعَجِّلُوا الْعَصْرَ، وَأَخِّرُوا الظُّهْرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abdah ibn Sulayman → Isma‘il → Hammad → Ibrahim → Al-Aswad → ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abdah ibn Sulayman narrated to us from Isma‘il, from Hammad, from Ibrahim, from Al-Aswad, from ‘Umar, who said: “If it is a cloudy day, hasten ‘Asr and delay Zuhr.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنُ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ بْنِ رُفَيْعٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: عَجِّلُوا صَلَاةَ النَّهَارِ فِي يَوْمِ الْغَيْمِ، وَأَخِّرُوا الْمَغْرِبَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Hasan ibn Salih → ‘Abd al-‘Aziz ibn Rufay‘
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Hasan ibn Salih narrated to us from ‘Abd al-‘Aziz ibn Rufay‘, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “Hasten the day prayer on a cloudy day, and delay Maghrib.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا قَيْسٌ، عَنْ أَبِي حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ خَزَّامِ بْنِ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ، يَقُولُ: إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْغَيْمِ، فَعَجِّلُوا الظُّهْرَ، وَأَخِّرُوا الْعَصْرَ، وَأَخِّرُوا الْمَغْرِبَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Qays → Abu Husayn → Khazzam ibn Jabir
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Qays narrated to us from Abu Husayn, from Khazzam ibn Jabir, who said: I heard Ibn Mas‘ud say: “If it is a cloudy day, hasten Zuhr, delay ‘Asr, and delay Maghrib.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْأَوْزَاعِيُّ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُهَاجِرِ، عَنْ بُرَيْدَةَ الْأَسْلَمِيِّ، قَالَ: كُنَّا مَعَهُ فِي غُزَاةٍ، فَقَالَ: سَمِعْتُ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ: بَكِّرُوا بِالصَّلَاةِ فِي يَوْمِ الْغَيْمِ، فَإِنَّهُ مَنْ فَاتَتْهُ صَلَاةُ الْعَصْرِ حَبِطَ عَمَلُهُ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Al-Awza‘i → Yahya ibn Abi Kathir → Abu Qilabah → Abu al-Muhajir → Buraydah al-Aslami
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-Awza‘i narrated to us from Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, from Abu Qilabah, from Abu al-Muhajir, from Buraydah al-Aslami, who said: We were with him in a raid, and he said: I heard the Prophet ﷺ say: “Perform the prayer early on a cloudy day, for whoever misses the ‘Asr prayer, his deeds are nullified.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا هِشَامٌ الدَّسْتُوَائِيُّ، عَنْ يَحْيَى، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمَلِيحِ، عَنْ بُرَيْدَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، بِنَحْوٍ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Yazid ibn Harun → Hisham al-Dastuwa’i → Yahya → Abu Qilabah → Abu al-Malih → Buraydah
english.text: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, saying: Hisham al-Dastuwa’i informed us from Yahya, from Abu Qilabah, from Abu al-Malih, from Buraydah, from the Prophet ﷺ, with something similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ بَكْرِ بْنِ مَاعِزٍ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ لِمُؤَذِّنِهِ: إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْغَيْمِ، فَأَغْسِقْ بِالْمَغْرِبِ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Yunus ibn Abi Ishaq → Bakr ibn Ma‘iz → Al-Rabi‘ ibn Khuthaym
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Yunus ibn Abi Ishaq narrated to us from Bakr ibn Ma‘iz, from Al-Rabi‘ ibn Khuthaym, that he said to his Mu’adhdhin: “If it is a cloudy day, delay the Maghrib prayer until it is dark.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يُعْجِبُهُ فِي يَوْمِ الْغَيْمِ أَنْ يُؤَخِّرَ الظُّهْرَ، وَيُعَجِّلَ الْعَصْرَ

english.narrator: Abu Usamah → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Usamah narrated to us from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, who said: “He used to like delaying Zuhr and hastening ‘Asr on a cloudy day.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، قَالَ: يُعَجَّلُ الْعَصْرُ يَوْمَ الْغَيْمِ، وَيُؤَخَّرُ الْمَغْرِبُ

english.narrator: Ibn Yaman → Sufyan → Al-A‘mash → Abu Salih
english.text: Ibn Yaman narrated to us from Sufyan, from Al-A‘mash, from Abu Salih, who said: “‘Asr is hastened on a cloudy day, and Maghrib is delayed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: يُعَجَّلُ الْعَصْرُ، وَيُؤَخَّرُ الْمَغْرِبُ

english.narrator: Ibn Yaman → Sufyan → Abu Hamzah → Ibrahim
english.text: Ibn Yaman narrated to us from Sufyan, from Abu Hamzah, from Ibrahim, who said: “‘Asr is hastened, and Maghrib is delayed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ، بِمِثْلِهِ

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Yaman → Sufyan → Khalid → Al-Hasan/Ibn Sirin
english.text: Yahya ibn Yaman narrated to us from Sufyan, from Khalid, from Al-Hasan and Ibn Sirin, with similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} [الذاريات: 17] قَالَ: لَا يَنَامُونَ عَنِ الْعِشَاءِ الْآخِرَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs ibn Ghiyath → ‘Asim → Abu al-‘Aliyah
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us, saying: Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated to us from ‘Asim, from Abu al-‘Aliyah, regarding: {They used to sleep but little of the night} [Al-Dhariyat: 17], he said: “They would not sleep before the ‘Isha prayer.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ وَبِالْأَسْحَارِ هُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ} [الذاريات: 18] قَالَ: صَلَّوْا، فَلَمَّا كَانَ السَّحَرُ اسْتَغْفَرُوا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Mubarak → Al-Hasan
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Mubarak, from Al-Hasan, regarding: {They used to sleep but little of the night, and in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness} [Al-Dhariyat: 17-18], he said: “They prayed, and when dawn came they asked for forgiveness.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رَوَاحَةَ، {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} [الذاريات: 17] قَالَ: هَجَعُوا قَلِيلًا، ثُمَّ مَدُّوهَا إِلَى السَّحَرِ

english.narrator: Humayd → Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid → A man → Al-Hasan → ‘Abd Allah ibn Rawahah
english.text: Humayd narrated to us from Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid, from a man, from Al-Hasan, from ‘Abd Allah ibn Rawahah, regarding: {They used to sleep but little of the night} [Al-Dhariyat: 17], he said: “They slept a little, then extended it until dawn.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} [الذاريات: 17] قَالَ: ذَلِكَ إِذْ أُمِرُوا بِقِيَامِ اللَّيْلِ وَكَانَ أَبُو ذَرٍّ يَحْتَجِزُ احْتِجَازَهُ، وَيَأْخُذُ الْعَصَا فَيَعْتَمِدُ عَلَيْهَا، فَكَانُوا كَذَلِكَ حَتَّى أُنْزِلَتِ الرُّخْصَةُ: {فَاقْرَءُوا مَا تَيَسَّرَ مِنْهُ}

english.narrator: Ghundar → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, regarding: {They used to sleep but little of the night} [Al-Dhariyat: 17], he said: “That was when they were commanded to stand at night.” Abu Dharr used to tie his waist wrapper tightly and take a staff to lean on. They were like that until the concession was revealed: {So recite what is easy [for you] of it} [Al-Muzzammil: 20].

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بُكَيْرُ بْنُ أَبِي السُّمَّيْطِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا قَتَادَةُ، فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} [الذاريات: 17] قَالَ: "كَانَ الْحَسَنُ يَقُولُ: قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَنَامُونَ وَكَانَ مُطَرِّفُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يَقُولُ: كَانُوا قَلَّ لَيْلَةٍ إِلَّا يُصِيبُونَ مِنْهَا وَكَانَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ يَقُولُ:" لَا يَنَامُونَ حَتَّى يُصَلُّوا الْعَتَمَةَ

english.narrator: ‘Affan → Bukayr ibn Abi al-Sumayt → Qatadah
english.text: ‘Affan narrated to us, saying: Bukayr ibn Abi al-Sumayt narrated to us, saying: Qatadah narrated to us regarding His saying: {They used to sleep but little of the night} [Al-Dhariyat: 17]. He said: Al-Hasan used to say: “They slept little at night.” Mutarrif ibn ‘Abd Allah used to say: “Few nights passed without them obtaining [some portion of prayer] from them.” And Muhammad ibn ‘Ali used to say: “They would not sleep until they prayed the ‘Atamah (‘Isha).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الشِّخِّيرِ، قَالَ: قَلَّ لَيْلَةٍ أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ هَجَعُوهَا كُلَّهَا

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Sa‘id → Qatadah → Mutarrif ibn ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Shikhkhir
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Sa‘id, from Qatadah, from Mutarrif ibn ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Shikhkhir, who said: “Rarely did a night pass for them in which they slept all of it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ بِسْطَامٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، قَالَ: الْمُتَّقِينَ هُمُ الْقَلِيلُ

english.narrator: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah → Ibn Bistam → Al-Dahhak
english.text: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Ibn Bistam, from Al-Dahhak, who said: “The Muttaqun (pious) are the few.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} [الذاريات: 17] قَالَ: هَجَعُوا قَلِيلًا، ثُمَّ مَدُّوهَا إِلَى السَّحَرِ

english.narrator: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah → Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid → A man → Al-Hasan
english.text: Marwan ibn Mu‘awiyah narrated to us from Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid, from a man, from Al-Hasan, regarding: {They used to sleep but little of the night} [Al-Dhariyat: 17], he said: “They slept a little, then extended it until dawn.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: قَلَّ لَيْلَةً أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ هَجَعُوهَا

english.narrator: Yahya ibn Sa‘id → ‘Awf → Sa‘id ibn Abi al-Hasan
english.text: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us from ‘Awf, from Sa‘id ibn Abi al-Hasan, who said: “Rarely did a night pass for them in which they slept.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: كَانُوا لَا يَنَامُونَ كُلَّ اللَّيْلِ

english.narrator: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman → Layth → Mujahid
english.text: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman narrated to us from Layth, from Mujahid, who said: “They used not to sleep the entire night.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} [الذاريات: 17] قَالَ: قَلَّ لَيْلَةً تَمُرُّ بِهِمْ إِلَّا صَلَّوْا فِيهَا

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Ibn Abi Layla → Al-Hakam → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Ibn Abi Layla, from Al-Hakam, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Abbas, regarding: {They used to sleep but little of the night} [Al-Dhariyat: 17], he said: “Rarely did a night pass for them except that they prayed during it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ عَدِيٍّ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، قَالَ: كَانُوا مِنَ النَّاسِ قَلِيلٌ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Al-Zubayr ibn ‘Adi → Al-Dahhak
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Al-Zubayr ibn ‘Adi, from Al-Dahhak, who said: “They were few among the people.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، {كَانُوا قَلِيلًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ} [الذاريات: 17] قَالَ: يَنَامُونَ

english.narrator: Waki‘ → Sufyan → Mansur → Ibrahim
english.text: Waki‘ narrated to us from Sufyan, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, regarding: {They used to sleep but little of the night} [Al-Dhariyat: 17], he said: “They sleep.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ كَانُوا قَلِيلًا يَنَامُونَ لَيْلَةً حَتَّى الصَّبَاحِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Abi Najih
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us from Ibn Abi Najih: “They used to sleep little at night until the morning.”
